5-437.q: The arrows to the left on sign [[a30]] mean:
5-437.a: There is a train station to your left.
5-437.b: The railway line runs alongside the road you are driving on, from the left.
5-437.c: The train is coming from your left.
5-437.d: You are approaching the railway crossing after bending or turning left.

5-438.q: Where do you stop your vehicle when you see sign [[e6]]?
5-438.b: Before the stop line or before the pedestrian crossing.
5-438.c: At the place where you can see pedestrian movement.
5-438.d: This sign is for pedestrians, not for vehicles.

5-439.q: How do you act when you see [[e8]] placed before the turn?
5-439.a: Proceed with caution, obeying the instructions of the signs if any.
5-439.b: Stop your vehicle before the stop line or before the pedestrian crossing.
5-439.c: This sign is for pedestrians, not for vehicles.
5-439.d: Stop your vehicle beside the nearest traffic light.

5-440.q: If a number is written on sign [[a29]], what does it mean?
5-440.a: The length of the railway crossing in meters.
5-440.b: The distance in meters to the railway crossing.
5-440.c: The maximum speed permitted before reaching the crossing.
5-440.d: The number of the regional road the train passes through.

5-441.q: How far is sign [[a38]] from the section where the road becomes two-way?
5-441.a: This sign is placed 50 to 100 m from the start of the section.
5-441.b: This sign is placed 150 m from the start of the section.
5-441.c: This sign is placed at the start of the section.
5-441.d: This sign is placed 100 m from the start of the section.

5-442.q: If sign [[b12]] is placed at the entrance of a road, does it also prohibit commercial vehicles from entering this road?
5-442.b: Yes, it also prohibits the entry of all commercial vehicles.
5-442.c: This sign prohibits the entry of private cars and public taxis only.
5-442.d: It prohibits only commercial vehicles whose gross weight does not exceed 4,000 kg.

5-443.q: If sign [[b13]] is placed at the entrance of a road, which types of motorcycles may not enter this road?
5-443.a: All types of motorcycles except those with an engine capacity of no more than 50 cc.
5-443.c: Only motorcycles traveling on three wheels.

5-444.q: Does sign [[b28]] end the effect of sign [[b29]]?
5-444.d: Only the nearest intersection ends its effect.

5-445.q: If sign [[b37]] is placed on the left side of the road, it obliges to stop:
5-445.a: Whoever wants to turn left and whoever wants to make a U-turn to the left.
5-445.b: Whoever wants to turn left only.
5-445.c: Whoever wants to turn in any direction.
5-445.d: Only whoever wants to make a U-turn to the left.

5-446.q: What ends the effect of sign [[b37]]?
5-446.d: Complying with the requirements of the sign.

5-447.q: Does sign [[b62]] prohibit the entry of motorcycles?
5-447.b: Yes, it prohibits the entry of motorcycles traveling on two wheels only.
5-447.c: Yes, it prohibits the entry of all motorcycles.
5-447.d: Yes, it prohibits the entry of motorcycles traveling on three wheels only.

5-448.q: On a road where sign [[b64]] is placed at its entrance, may pedestrians enter the shoulder of this road?
5-448.b: Pedestrians may enter provided they do not bring bicycles or animals with them.
5-448.c: Yes, because the sign prohibits pedestrians from entering the road, not the road shoulder.
5-448.d: Entry is prohibited only to the road shoulder on the same side where the sign is placed.

5-449.q: One of the differences between sign [[c7]] and sign [[b65]]?
5-449.a: The first is a path for pedestrians and the second is a lane for pedestrians.
5-449.b: There is no difference between them.
5-449.c: The first is placed off the road and the second on the road.
5-449.d: The first is placed on the road and the second off the road.

6-450.q: What ends the effect of sign [[c6]]?

6-451.q: If a place name on sign [[c28]] is painted in blue while the rest of the sign is green, this means:
6-451.b: That this place is reached by a wide road.
6-451.c: That this place is reached by the expressway.
6-451.d: The board may not be painted in two colors.

6-452.a: A parking space for the vehicle of a leg-disabled person.
6-452.b: A parking space for a disabled person's vehicle.

6-453.q: Which sign means that the number of lanes is decreasing?

6-454.a: A median at the edge of the road.
6-454.b: Marking of a public transport lane.
6-454.c: The boundary of the road edge.

6-455.a: A dense broken line before the appearance of a continuous dividing line.
6-455.b: A broken line before the start of a public transport lane.
6-455.c: A broken line before entering a road inside the cities.
6-455.d: A broken line before entering a one-way road.

6-456.q: When approaching a railway crossing while sign [[e11]] is operating but the barrier is open:
6-456.a: Only buses must stop, on a non-urban road.
6-456.b: The crossing may be passed carefully until it is closed by the barrier.
6-456.c: The crossing may be passed as long as we do not hear or see the train.
6-456.d: Drivers of all types of vehicles must stop before the crossing.

6-457.q: How does the driver recognize an expressway?
6-457.a: By the sign permitting a maximum speed of 110 km/h.

6-458.q: Which four of the following signs each prohibit the entry of motorcycles?

6-459.q: Which two of the following signs are considered auxiliary signs?

6-460.q: Are the auxiliary devices on the road considered traffic signs?
6-460.b: They are not considered traffic signs because they are not fixed and can be used by students.
6-460.c: They are not considered traffic signs because they are not signs.
6-460.d: They are considered traffic signs only if placed on the edge of the sidewalk.

6-461.q: When there are arrows above the traffic light:
6-461.a: Travel follows the arrows only when the traffic light is not operating.
6-461.b: Travel follows the arrows only when the traffic light is operating.
6-461.c: Travel follows only the arrows above the traffic light, in all cases.
6-461.d: These arrows are binding only if there are arrows on the road surface.

6-462.a: No entry for pedestrians, including the road shoulder.
6-462.b: No entry for pedestrians, except the road shoulder.
6-462.c: Pedestrians may not walk on the road shoulder only.
6-462.d: Pedestrians may not walk on the sidewalk only.

6-463.q: When you see sign [[b20]] showing the number 50:
6-463.a: All types of vehicles may exceed this speed.
6-463.b: All types of motor vehicles may exceed this speed.
6-463.c: No vehicle may travel at a speed exceeding the figure shown on the sign.
6-463.d: Slow vehicles may not exceed this speed.

6-464.a: You may only drive over this area without stopping on it.
6-464.b: Driving and parking on this area are prohibited.
6-464.c: You may stop on this area only to pick up or drop off passengers.
6-464.d: Driving on this area is absolutely prohibited.

6-465.q: When a driving-school vehicle, during instruction, enters an expressway with sign [[b53]] placed at its entrance:
6-465.a: It must travel at no less than the speed stated on the sign, unless there is a reasonable cause.
6-465.b: It must travel at no less than the speed stated on the sign only if it is a private car.
6-465.c: It is exempt from the duty to travel at the speed stated on the sign.
6-465.d: It must travel at no more than the speed stated on the sign.

6-466.a: Driving slower than the figure stated on the sign is absolutely prohibited.
6-466.b: The entrance of a road inside the cities.
6-466.c: A road for motor vehicles that can and may travel at a speed higher than the figure stated on the sign.
6-466.d: A maximum permitted speed of 55 km/h.

6-467.b: Attention: the left lane is closed ahead.
6-467.c: Attention: a steep descent ahead, 150 m away.
6-467.d: A steep descent ahead; if a number is stated, it is the gradient percentage.

6-468.q: You are approaching the intersection and the traffic light shows a flashing green light:
6-468.a: This means pedestrians are crossing the intersection.
6-468.b: This means the traffic light has a fault.
6-468.c: This means the green light will become steady shortly.
6-468.d: This means the light will turn yellow then red shortly.

6-469.a: Approaching a railway crossing 170 m away; if a number is stated, it means the distance in meters to the crossing.
6-469.b: Approaching a railway crossing, 100 m away.
6-469.c: Approaching a railway crossing, 250 m away.
6-469.d: Attention: works on the sides of the road.

6-470.q: Point to the sign that means a yellow light:

6-471.a: Stop for a moment to make sure there are no children on the road.
6-471.b: Use the horn to alert the children on the road.
6-471.c: Slow down and drive carefully, because there are pedestrians near this area.
6-471.d: Do not increase your speed, because there are children near this area.

6-472.q: When the traffic light shows red and there is no stop line or pedestrian crossing, where do you stop?
6-472.a: There is no traffic light without a pedestrian crossing or a stop line.
6-472.b: At a place from which I can see the crossing traffic.
6-472.c: The place is not important; what matters is stopping at the red light.
6-472.d: Beside the nearest pole carrying the nearest traffic light.

6-473.a: Guide lines for turning at intersections.
6-473.b: Intersection arms leading to one-way roads.
6-473.c: Turning right or left is prohibited at the intersection ahead.
6-473.d: Only a right turn is permitted at such an intersection.

6-474.a: A pedestrian crossing; if it is divided into two parts, pedestrians must cross on the right-hand part of the crossing.
6-474.c: A pedestrian crossing on a two-way road if it is doubled.

6-475.q: May a vehicle other than a public taxi drop off passengers immediately after sign [[b58]]?
6-475.a: Yes, it is permitted for the public bus only.
6-475.d: A vehicle other than a taxi may only pick up passengers.

6-476.q: If broken lines are marked on the road surface - sign [[d1]]:
6-476.b: Drivers of vehicles and animals must use the far right lane of these lines.
6-476.c: Drivers may not drive the vehicle or part of it on these lines except to overtake.
6-476.d: Drivers may not drive the vehicle or part of it on these lines except to comply with the law.

6-477.q: If a continuous dividing line is marked on the road surface - sign [[d4]]:
6-477.b: This line may be crossed if it is to the right of a broken line.
6-477.c: Only animal riders may cross this line.
6-477.d: This line may not be crossed in any case.

6-478.q: The driver may not deviate from their lane:
6-478.a: If doing so would obstruct traffic or endanger others.
6-478.b: When sign [[c4]] is placed at the beginning of the road.

6-479.a: A railway crossing with more than one track, 100 m away.
6-479.c: A railway crossing with more than one track, 10 m away.
6-479.d: A railway crossing with more than one track, 4 m away.

7-480.b: A pedestrian crossing ahead, 100 m away.
7-480.c: A path for pedestrians on the road.
7-480.d: A pedestrian crossing ahead, 150 m away.

7-481.b: Go right or left from behind the sign only.
7-481.c: Pass the obstacle ahead of you on the right side or on the left side.
7-481.d: Go right or left from in front of the sign only.

7-482.q: May a driving-school vehicle enter the expressway during driving instruction?

7-484.a: A junction ahead - roads branching off to the right and to the left.
7-484.d: A branch to the left, then to the right.

7-485.a: The road has room for fifty vehicles only.
7-485.b: Driving slower than 50 km/h is prohibited.
7-485.c: Driving faster than the figure stated on the sign is prohibited.
7-485.d: You are on a road inside the cities.

7-486.b: Turn left in front of the sign.
7-486.c: The road on your left is a one-way road.
7-486.d: Go right from behind the sign.

7-487.b: The road ahead has two lanes.
7-487.c: There is a bridge over the middle of the road.
7-487.d: The lanes on the road ahead increase to two.

7-488.a: A public bus stop in the form of a bay.
7-488.b: The entrance to a yard; parking on this area is prohibited.
7-488.c: A stopping place for certain types of vehicles.
7-488.d: An angled parking place for vehicles.

7-489.c: Go straight ahead or right only.
7-489.d: On your left is a one-way road.

7-490.q: Which sign indicates the end of an urban road?

7-491.b: A one-way road ahead, 150 m away.
7-491.c: Attention: a tunnel ahead, 150 m away.
7-491.d: The road ahead has one lane only.

7-492.q: When sign [[e11]] is operating and there is no stop line or barrier, you must stop at a distance of:

7-493.a: The number of lanes is increasing.
7-493.b: Turning left leads to a parking place.
7-493.c: The continuous white line to the left of the broken lines may be crossed.
7-493.d: Broken rectangular lines indicating a change of lane direction for turning left.

7-494.a: A barrier before a railway crossing.
7-494.b: No stopping beside the curb except for vehicle types permitted to stop by a sign.
7-494.c: No parking or stopping beside the curb.
7-494.d: Parking beside the curb is permitted with cards.

7-495.a: No entry for all types of vehicles in this direction.
7-495.c: No entry for motor vehicles only in this direction.
7-495.d: No entry for all types of vehicles from both directions.

7-496.a: Driving faster than 50 km/h is prohibited.
7-496.b: End of the road section with the speed restriction.
7-496.c: The road ahead is 50 km long.

7-497.a: Stop and give right of way to the crossing traffic ahead of you.
7-497.b: Give right of way to the traffic ahead of you.
7-497.c: Slow down and give right of way to the crossing traffic ahead of you.
7-497.d: Stop to give right of way only if there are crossing vehicles.

7-498.b: The road becomes a single lane ahead.

7-499.b: A crossroads to the right and to the left ahead.
7-499.d: Right of way belongs to oncoming traffic.

7-500.a: Stop until you receive permission to continue traveling, or until the sign permitting travel appears.
7-500.b: Stop at the stop line until the train passes.
7-500.c: Stop and give right of way to the crossing traffic ahead of you.
7-500.d: A stop sign placed at the junctions of side streets.

7-501.q: Are the markings drawn on the road surface binding?
7-501.a: That depends on the color of the marking.
7-501.b: Some are binding and some are not.

7-502.q: You are driving on a road with one lane in each direction separated by a continuous dividing line, and ahead of you travels an animal-drawn cart:
7-502.a: You continue driving slowly behind the cart.
7-502.c: You overtake if there is a broken line to the left of the continuous line.
7-502.d: You overtake if there is a broken line on your right.

7-503.b: A sign that appears inside the green light of the traffic signal when it lights up.
7-503.c: The railway crossing may be passed after the train has gone by.
7-503.d: A sign indicating the green wave between traffic lights.

7-504.q: The traffic light shows green, and above it sign [[b46]] is placed:
7-504.a: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made when the traffic light shows red.
7-504.b: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the traffic light is not operating.
7-504.c: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may not be made.
7-504.d: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made.

7-505.q: The traffic light shows green, and above it sign [[b44]] is placed:
7-505.a: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made.
7-505.b: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may not be made.
7-505.c: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only if the intersection is clear.
7-505.d: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the traffic light is not operating.

7-506.q: The traffic light shows green, and above it sign [[b43]] is placed:
7-506.a: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may not be made.
7-506.b: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made.
7-506.c: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the red light appears in the traffic signal.
7-506.d: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the traffic light is not operating.

7-507.q: The traffic light shows green, and above it sign [[b41]] is placed:
7-507.a: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made.
7-507.b: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may not be made.
7-507.c: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the red light appears in the traffic signal.
7-507.d: A horseshoe (U) turn to the left may be made only when the traffic light is not operating.

7-508.q: When approaching a railway crossing, seeing sign [[a30]], and a slow vehicle is traveling in front of you:
7-508.a: Trucks with a gross weight over 4,000 kg may not overtake.
7-508.c: Overtaking is prohibited in all cases.
7-508.d: Overtaking is permitted when the barrier is open.

7-509.a: An area on the road where parking is prohibited.
7-509.b: A parking place for trucks for loading and unloading.
7-509.c: A barrier before a vehicle parking yard.
7-509.d: A private parking space for a doctor's car.

8-510.q: What do the following markings [[d14]] drawn on the road surface mean?
8-510.a: Stopping on these islands is permitted only to pick up and drop off passengers.
8-510.b: Traffic islands of various shapes; drivers of vehicles or animals may not drive or stop on them.
8-510.c: A parking place for private passenger cars and dual-purpose vehicles.
8-510.d: Parking on these islands is prohibited, but driving on them is possible.

8-511.q: May someone riding or leading an animal travel or stand on these islands [[d14]]?
8-511.a: The animal may stand on them until the road is clear of traffic.
8-511.b: The animal may walk on them only for the purpose of crossing the road.
8-511.d: These islands are intended to protect pedestrians and animals.

8-514.q: What ends the effect of sign [[b48]]?
8-514.c: Complying with the requirements of the sign.

8-515.q: If there is a yellow arrow on sign [[b49]], this means:
8-515.a: The yellow arrow indicates that the road is under repair.
8-515.b: The yellow arrow indicates a direction of travel for public transport only.
8-515.c: There may not be a yellow arrow on the mentioned sign.
8-515.d: The yellow arrow indicates the road margin.

8-516.q: Where must the driver drive when the road is bounded by the yellow line, sign [[d7]]?
8-516.a: The driver must drive on the left side of the line beside them.
8-516.b: The driver must drive on the right side of the line beside them.
8-516.c: The driver must drive on the right side of the line beside them, except when picking up and dropping off passengers.
8-516.d: The driver may drive on the left side of the line beside them or on its right side.

8-517.q: When the driver sees the green light in the traffic signal, sign [[e3]]:
8-517.a: They may travel in all directions unless there is a guidance sign above the traffic signal indicating otherwise.
8-517.b: They may travel in all directions unless there is a warning sign above the traffic signal indicating otherwise.
8-517.c: They may travel straight ahead only.
8-517.d: They may travel straight ahead and to the right only.

8-518.a: Merging of a road that has right of way with a side road from the right; turning left is prohibited.
8-518.b: Merging of a road with a side road that has right of way; turning right is prohibited.
8-518.c: Merging of a road that has right of way with a side road from the left; turning into the side road is prohibited.
8-518.d: Merging with a road that has right of way; turning left is prohibited.

8-519.a: Low-flying aircraft noise, 150 m from the airport boundary.
8-519.b: Low-flying aircraft noise, 150 m from the nearest runway.

8-521.q: If you want to park your vehicle at an angle on the road:
8-521.a: You may do so when there are lines on the road surface permitting it.
8-521.b: You may do so only in a vehicle parking bay.
8-521.c: You may do so if it does not obstruct traffic.
8-521.d: You may do so if the vehicle is a private car.

8-522.q: You are driving on an uphill single-lane road and a truck approaches from the opposite direction:
8-522.a: You have right of way, but it is advisable to be considerate of the truck.
8-522.b: Right of way belongs to the descending vehicle in all cases.
8-522.c: In high mountains, priority always belongs to the private car.
8-522.d: In high mountains, priority always belongs to the heavy vehicle.

8-523.q: When approaching a railway crossing and the barrier is open:
8-523.a: The driver must slow down in all cases.
8-523.b: The driver must stop if driving a bus.
8-523.c: The driver must stop in all cases at a distance of 4 m from the nearest rail.
8-523.d: The driver must stop if driving a bus on a road inside the cities only.

8-524.q: Is it permitted to pick up and drop off passengers on the road shoulder?
8-524.b: Yes, except on roads outside the cities.
8-524.d: Yes, except on expressways and roads outside the cities.

8-525.q: In which cases must the driver give a hand signal?
8-525.a: When the signaling devices develop a fault.
8-525.c: When driving a vehicle that is not exempt from the duty to install a signaling device.
8-525.d: When the circumstances do not appear to justify it.

8-526.q: Night, in the eyes of the law, is:
8-526.a: The period beginning a quarter of an hour before sunset and ending a quarter of an hour before sunrise.
8-526.b: The period beginning a quarter of an hour after sunset and ending a quarter of an hour after sunrise.
8-526.c: The period beginning a quarter of an hour after sunset and ending a quarter of an hour before sunrise.
8-526.d: The period beginning a quarter of an hour before sunset and ending a quarter of an hour after sunrise.

8-527.q: A vehicle with a minor fault may remain parked on the road:
8-527.b: For a maximum of seven days.

8-528.q: What is the correct order of precedence to be obeyed?
8-528.a: The police officer - the traffic light - the sign - the law.
8-528.b: The police officer - the traffic light except the flashing yellow light - the sign - the law.
8-528.c: The police officer - the traffic light except the flashing yellow light - the law - the sign.
8-528.d: The traffic light - the police officer - the law - the sign.

8-529.q: The light-switching device (dimmer) in the vehicle enables the driver to:
8-529.a: Switch the high beam to the road light and vice versa.
8-529.b: Switch the low (dipped) beam to the low light and vice versa.
8-529.c: Switch the high (road) beam to the low (dipped) beam and vice versa.
8-529.d: Switch the road light to the high beam and vice versa.

8-530.q: A driver involved in a road accident that resulted in a person's death or injury must:
8-530.a: Move the vehicle according to the instructions of the injured person or their companion.
8-530.b: Move the vehicle and park it at the side of the road in all cases.
8-530.c: Move the vehicle only if it obstructs traffic.
8-530.d: Stop the vehicle immediately at or near the accident site and not move it from its place.

8-531.q: How do you recognize an expressway?
8-531.a: By the median running along the road.
8-531.c: By the signs prohibiting the entry of pedestrians and bicycles.

8-532.q: A left turn from a two-way road into a two-way road with two or more lanes in each direction is made:
8-532.a: From as close as possible to the center of the road, into the left lane of our direction of travel.
8-532.b: From as close as possible to the center of the road, in a wide arc to the right.
8-532.c: From as close as possible to the center of the road, into the right lane of our direction of travel.
8-532.d: From as close as possible to the center of the road, to the far left.

8-533.q: When you notice an animal on the road:
8-533.b: You must use the horn before passing it.
8-533.c: You must not increase your speed.

8-534.q: The driver must stop the vehicle before a railway crossing inside the cities equipped with a barrier, when the barrier is open:
8-534.a: If the vehicle is carrying flammable materials.
8-534.b: If the vehicle is carrying more than 7 passengers besides the driver.
8-534.c: There is no such duty on the driver if the barrier is open.
8-534.d: This duty applies to bus drivers only.

8-535.q: When a vehicle is parked on the right side close to the road edge and its driver wants to start traveling:
8-535.a: The driver of any vehicle who wants to start traveling must signal, even without intending to change lanes.
8-535.b: Only a bus driver must signal after stopping at the station.
8-535.c: The duty to signal applies only to drivers who want to turn right or left.
8-535.d: The driver does not have to signal.

8-536.q: Lighting-up time, in the eyes of the law, is:
8-536.a: Night, and any other time when visibility is poor.
8-536.b: The period beginning a quarter of an hour before sunset and ending a quarter of an hour before sunrise.
8-536.c: The period beginning a quarter of an hour after sunset and ending an hour after sunrise.
8-536.d: The period from 1/11 of each year until 31/3 of the following year.

8-537.a: A line drawn across the road or part of it, marking the limit where a vehicle must stop.
8-537.b: A line across the road indicating the end of a pedestrian crossing.
8-537.c: A hand-shaped symbol inside an octagonal sign.
8-537.d: A line indicating the merging or meeting of two or more roads near a traffic light.

8-538.q: A driver involved in a road accident that resulted only in damage to another person's property must:
8-538.a: Clear the road for the free flow of traffic if the vehicle obstructs or is likely to obstruct traffic.
8-538.b: Not move the vehicle according to the orders of the owner of the damaged property.
8-538.c: Move the vehicle and tow it to a yard for damaged vehicles.
8-538.d: Stop the vehicle immediately at or near the accident site and not move it from its place.

8-539.q: When driving near schools, playgrounds, or children:
8-539.a: Do not increase your speed and increase your caution.
8-539.b: Slow down and move to the left lane.
8-539.c: Use the horn and do not increase your speed.
8-539.d: Slow down and increase your caution.

9-540.q: Are the signals given by a road worker binding on the driver?
9-540.a: No, because the worker is not a police officer.
9-540.b: Yes, when using the 'stop' and 'go' signals at the barrier.
9-540.d: Yes, if the worker is wearing an official uniform.

9-541.q: On a steep downhill road we must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us:
9-541.b: Only if the road is wet.
9-541.c: Only if the speed is high.

9-542.q: How does a driver turn right from a one-way road into a one-way road?
9-542.a: From as far right as possible into the far right.
9-542.b: From as far left as possible into the far right.
9-542.c: From as far left as possible into the far left.
9-542.d: From as far right as possible into the far left.

9-543.q: On a downhill road, right of way belongs to the ascending vehicles:
9-543.a: In all cases, unless the descending vehicle is a truck.
9-543.c: If the circumstances require one of them to stop.
9-543.d: In all cases, unless the ascending vehicle is a two-wheeled motorcycle.

9-544.q: Which of the following statements is correct?
9-544.a: No one may fill a motor vehicle's fuel tank while the engine is running.
9-544.b: Only the driver of a vehicle carrying more than 11 passengers may not fill its fuel tank while the engine is running.
9-544.c: The driver of a private car may fill its fuel tank while the engine is running.
9-544.d: Only the bus driver may not fill the fuel tank while the engine is running.

9-545.q: The high (road) beam of the vehicle illuminates at night a distance of:
9-545.a: No less than 100 m in front of the vehicle.
9-545.b: No less than 30 m in front of the vehicle.
9-545.c: No less than 15 m in front of the vehicle.
9-545.d: No more than 100 m in front of the vehicle.

9-546.q: The low (dipped/passing) beam of the vehicle illuminates at night a distance of:
9-546.a: No less than 30 m in front of the vehicle.
9-546.b: No less than 15 m in front of the vehicle.
9-546.c: No more than 30 m in front of the vehicle.
9-546.d: No less than 100 m in front of the vehicle.

9-547.q: The reversing light illuminates the road behind the vehicle for a distance of:

9-548.q: May a vehicle be repaired on the road or in a public parking area?
9-548.b: It is permitted provided the vehicle does not remain in the same place for more than 24 hours.
9-548.c: It is permitted provided the repair place is not adjacent to a garage or vehicle repair workshop.
9-548.d: It is permitted provided it does not disturb road users.

9-549.q: A warning lamp is installed in the motor vehicle in front of the driver's seat, which lights up when the high beam is on, in the color:

9-550.q: How do you make a left turn into a one-way road?
9-550.a: Into the right lane in the direction of travel.
9-550.b: To as close as possible to the center of the road.
9-550.c: To the left side of the road in every case.
9-550.d: In a wide arc to the right side of the road.

9-551.q: May a vehicle be parked on the sidewalk?
9-551.a: It is permitted when the vehicle has a fault that prevents it from continuing.
9-551.b: A vehicle may be parked on the sidewalk if the sidewalk is more than 3 m wide.
9-551.c: It is permitted for cargo vehicles only, for loading and unloading.
9-551.d: A vehicle may not be parked on the sidewalk unless there is a sign permitting it.

9-552.a: Only if the road has three or five lanes.
9-552.b: Only on a two-way road with two or more lanes in each direction.
9-552.c: Only if the road conditions and traffic allow it.
9-552.d: Only on a one-way road with more than two lanes.

9-553.q: Use of the road must be in a manner that:
9-553.a: Allows reaching the destination by the shortest route and quickly.
9-553.b: Does not obstruct traffic and does not endanger anyone.

9-554.q: When the vehicle develops a fault, the warning triangle must be placed:
9-554.a: On the rear window of the vehicle.
9-554.b: Behind the vehicle so that a driver approaching from behind can see the triangle from a distance of at least 100 m.
9-554.c: The distance is not important; what matters is that it is behind the vehicle.
9-554.d: Behind the vehicle at a distance of at least 100 m.

9-555.q: The definition of a lane is:
9-555.b: Any road wide enough for more than two vehicles.
9-555.c: A part of the road width sufficient for one line of vehicles traveling on more than two wheels.
9-555.d: The road designated for one type of road user.

9-556.a: Any structure or traffic island dividing the road along its length.
9-556.b: The continuous yellow line at the road edge.
9-556.c: The white lines drawn across the road for pedestrians to cross.
9-556.d: The continuous white line in the middle of the road.

9-557.q: Parking is prohibited before a stop line within:

9-558.q: If a vehicle stops before a pedestrian crossing to fulfill its duty:
9-558.a: It may be overtaken if the road is one-way.
9-558.c: It must be overtaken with extreme caution.
9-558.d: The horn must be used when overtaking it to alert the pedestrians.

9-559.q: When a vehicle parks in a permitted place beside the curb:
9-559.a: The distance is not important; what matters is not to park at an angle unless there are markings permitting it.
9-559.b: The distance between the curb and the nearest wheels must be no less than 40 cm.
9-559.c: The distance between the curb and the nearest wheels must not exceed 40 cm.
9-559.d: The distance between the curb and the nearest wheels must be exactly 40 cm.

9-560.q: When you exit from a vehicle parking area onto the road:
9-560.a: Give right of way to traffic on the road crossing ahead of you.
9-560.b: Give right of way only if there is a stop sign in front of you.
9-560.c: Give right of way only to vehicles coming from your right.
9-560.d: You have right of way if you are on the right of the road.

9-561.q: In which direction is the steering wheel turned when you want to park the vehicle on a descent?
9-561.a: The front wheels must be straight when parking a vehicle on a descent.
9-561.b: The direction of the steering wheel is not important when the vehicle is parked.
9-561.c: The steering wheel is turned toward the center of the road.
9-561.d: The steering wheel is turned toward the sidewalk or the road edge.

9-562.q: Who is responsible, in the eyes of the law, for the use of seat belts by the passengers in the vehicle?
9-562.d: The vehicle owner and its driver.

9-563.q: You may not overtake another vehicle:
9-563.a: When the other vehicle belongs to the police or security forces.
9-563.b: When the overtaking would obstruct the other vehicle or endanger it.
9-563.c: When the other vehicle is a bus operating on a service line.
9-563.d: When the other vehicle is a slow vehicle traveling on the road shoulder.

9-564.q: On a narrow two-way road that is not wide enough for two vehicles to pass:
9-564.a: Right of way belongs to the oncoming heavy vehicles.
9-564.b: Both oncoming vehicles must move to the far right, even down onto the road shoulder.
9-564.c: A road not wide enough for two vehicles cannot be a two-way road.
9-564.d: Stop and give right of way to the oncoming vehicles.

9-565.q: When must the warning triangle be placed?
9-565.a: When the vehicle breaks down during daylight hours only.
9-565.b: In every case where the vehicle stops due to a fault.
9-565.c: Only when the vehicle breaks down and is left unattended.
9-565.d: Only when the vehicle breaks down on a road inside the cities.

9-566.q: A driver who parks their vehicle and leaves it on an uphill road with a curb must turn the front wheels:
9-566.c: Toward the center of the road.

9-567.a: Within 15 m before and after a bus stop, on both sides of the road, unless the road is 12 m wide or more.
9-567.b: Within the limits of a bus stop.
9-567.c: Within 12 m before and after a bus stop.
9-567.d: Within 12 m before and after the sign indicating a bus stop.

9-568.q: In which cases is driving on the sidewalk permitted?
9-568.a: If the lane you are traveling in is blocked, on a two-way road with one lane in each direction marked with a continuous dividing line.
9-568.d: To enter or exit a yard or square.

9-569.q: A left turn from a two-way road divided by a continuous dividing line is made:
9-569.a: From the far right of the road.
9-569.b: From the place most comfortable for the driver.
9-569.c: From the far left of the road.
9-569.d: From as close as possible to the continuous dividing line.

10-570.q: May a median (dividing area) be crossed?
10-570.b: Yes, it may, to cross to the other part of the road.
10-570.c: Yes, it may, to make a horseshoe (U) turn.
10-570.d: It is permitted only for an animal-drawn cart.

10-571.q: If a police officer orders you to enter a road closed to vehicle traffic:
10-571.a: Turn around and enter the road from the other side.
10-571.c: The police officer's order must be obeyed in this case.
10-571.d: Enter the road only if a police vehicle enters ahead of you.

10-572.q: Who must act with caution on the road?
10-572.d: Only drivers of private cars in general and truck drivers in particular.

10-573.q: A vehicle may be parked on the left side of the road:
10-573.b: On a one-way road with a sign at its entrance indicating that it is a one-way road.
10-573.c: On a one-way road divided by a median along its length.
10-573.d: Only if the road is more than 12 m wide.

10-574.q: What safety measures do you take to avoid hitting the vehicle traveling in front of you?
10-574.a: Keep a sufficient distance from the vehicle in front of you.
10-574.b: Drive faster than the vehicle in front of you.
10-574.c: Drive at the same speed as the vehicle in front of you.
10-574.d: Drive at twice the speed of the vehicle in front of you.

10-575.q: May a person drive a vehicle while under the influence of sedative drugs?
10-575.a: It is permitted only for the purpose of receiving treatment.
10-575.c: Only with the permission of a police officer.
10-575.d: It is permitted only for the purpose of transporting an injured person.

10-576.a: A road where traffic moves in one direction, or a road alongside a median.
10-576.b: A road where traffic moves in two directions.
10-576.c: A road with only one lane in each direction.
10-576.d: Any road with more than two lanes in the same direction.

10-577.q: Is it permitted to give a hand signal?
10-577.a: Yes, if the vehicle's signaling device breaks down.
10-577.b: No, putting your hand out of the vehicle is prohibited.
10-577.d: Yes, a hand signal must be given in every case.

10-578.q: On a two-way road divided into three lanes:
10-578.a: Driving in the right lane is not permitted.
10-578.b: Driving in the middle lane is absolutely not permitted.
10-578.c: Driving in the right lane is permitted for overtaking.
10-578.d: Driving in the middle lane is permitted for overtaking.

10-579.q: When we park the vehicle on an uphill road without a curb:
10-579.a: We point the front wheels straight ahead.
10-579.b: The direction of the front wheels is not important.
10-579.c: We point the front wheels toward the center of the road.
10-579.d: We point the front wheels toward the edge of the road.

10-580.q: At an intersection, right of way belongs to the vehicle coming from the right:
10-580.a: Only when there is a stop sign at the intersection.
10-580.b: When there is a traffic light at the intersection.
10-580.c: When there are no signs indicating otherwise.

10-581.q: A left turn from a two-way road into a two-way road with one lane in each direction is made:
10-581.a: From as close as possible to the center of the road, in a wide arc to the left side.
10-581.b: From as close as possible to the center of the road, to the far left.
10-581.c: From the far right into the far right.
10-581.d: From as close as possible to the center of the road, in a wide arc to the right side.

10-582.a: When passing a bus stopped at the station.

10-583.b: Overtaking must be done with caution.
10-583.c: The high beam must be used when overtaking.
10-583.d: The low beam must be used when overtaking.

10-584.q: On a downhill road, in a section that is not wide enough for two vehicles:
10-584.a: Right of way belongs to the ascending vehicles.
10-584.b: Right of way belongs to the heavy vehicles.
10-584.c: Right of way belongs to the vehicles on the right.
10-584.d: Right of way belongs to the descending vehicles.

10-585.a: To prevent a danger that cannot be avoided in any other way.
10-585.c: For the purpose of overtaking only.
10-585.d: For the purpose of suddenly increasing speed.

10-586.q: What are the duties of vehicle drivers toward an emergency vehicle?
10-586.a: Move their vehicles to the right of the road, slow down, and give the emergency vehicle right of way.
10-586.b: Move their vehicles to the right of the road, stop there, and give the emergency vehicle right of way.
10-586.c: Slow down to allow the emergency vehicle to pass.
10-586.d: Increase their speed so as not to obstruct the emergency vehicle.

10-587.q: If a vehicle stopped and gave right of way to an emergency vehicle, at what distance behind the emergency vehicle may it resume traveling?

10-588.q: When there is a broken line in the middle of the road:
10-588.a: Overtaking is permitted if the road is clear.
10-588.b: Overtaking is permitted for private cars only.
10-588.c: Overtaking is prohibited in all cases.
10-588.d: Overtaking is permitted in all cases.

10-589.q: To whom do you give right of way at an intersection without signs?
10-589.c: To vehicles on the wider road.
10-589.d: To vehicles coming from the right and approaching the intersection.

10-590.q: A road closed at both ends is considered, in the eyes of the law:
10-590.b: A place for pedestrians to walk, not a road.
10-590.d: A road in the eyes of the law.

10-591.q: When you park the vehicle on a descent with a curb, you use the parking brake (handbrake) and:
10-591.a: Point the front wheels toward the center of the road and use reverse gear.
10-591.b: Point the front wheels toward the center of the road and use first gear.
10-591.c: Point the front wheels toward the curb and use first gear.
10-591.d: Point the front wheels toward the curb and use reverse gear.

10-592.q: When driving near a group of children:
10-592.a: The horn must be used intermittently to alert them.
10-592.b: The horn must be used continuously to alert them.
10-592.c: You must slow down and increase your caution.
10-592.d: You must stop until the children pass.

10-593.q: If two vehicles collide and there are no injuries to people:
10-593.a: Their drivers must move the vehicles away and remove every hazardous material from the road.
10-593.b: Their drivers must not move their vehicles until the police arrive.
10-593.c: The order to move the vehicles must be received from a police officer.
10-593.d: Leave the vehicles in place and remove every hazardous material from the road.

10-594.q: When you want to start moving from the side of the road:
10-594.a: You have right of way only if you were parked on an uphill road.
10-594.b: You signal and drive off, because you have right of way.
10-594.c: You may start moving provided you give right of way to vehicles traveling in your direction and coming from behind.
10-594.d: You give right of way to the vehicle behind you only if you were parked on the left.

10-595.q: How do you act before a narrow bridge?
10-595.c: You continue without increasing speed and cross the bridge.
10-595.d: You use a sudden flash of the high beam.

10-596.q: The area of the road shoulder extends up to:
10-596.a: Three meters from the road edge, or up to the drainage channel.
10-596.c: One meter from the road edge.
10-596.d: Every area beside the road edge, whatever its width.

10-597.q: When a median divides the entire road into two parts:
10-597.a: Each part becomes a two-way road.
10-597.b: The two parts on either side of the median move in the same direction.
10-597.c: The type of road does not change at all.
10-597.d: Each part becomes a one-way road.

10-598.q: When do you use the high beam?
10-598.a: On roads outside the cities, and on roads inside the cities when the road is not properly lit.
10-598.c: On roads inside the cities only.

10-599.q: When reversing, you must take the necessary measures to prevent:

11-600.q: On a one-way road with more than two lanes:
11-600.a: You may use the left lane for turning right only.
11-600.b: You must drive in the right lane, use the left one when overtaking, or the far left when turning left.
11-600.c: You may use the left lane if the road is clear.
11-600.d: You may use the lane to the left of the far right lane to overtake an overtaking vehicle.

11-601.q: The driver uses the parking brake (handbrake):
11-601.a: Only when parking the vehicle on a sloping road.
11-601.b: To stop the vehicle from continuing to skid.
11-601.c: To secure the vehicle against moving while parked, or to stop it in an emergency.
11-601.d: To stop the vehicle from continuing to sink (get stuck).

11-602.q: When there are arrows on the road surface for going straight, right and left:
11-602.a: Moving from one lane to another is permitted after taking precautionary measures.
11-602.b: Moving from the left lane to the right lane only is permitted.
11-602.c: Moving from the straight-ahead lane to the right lane is permitted for overtaking or turning left.
11-602.d: Moving from one lane to another is not permitted.

11-603.q: What is the function of the turn signal (indicator) in the vehicle?
11-603.c: To strengthen the lighting when turning.
11-603.d: To make the vehicle noticeable from a distance, especially at night.

11-604.a: A motor vehicle designed for towing and for carrying out works.
11-604.b: A motor vehicle designed for carrying out works but not designed for towing.
11-604.c: A motor vehicle designed for towing or for being transported on another vehicle.
11-604.d: A mobile compressor or mobile electricity generator, towed by a commercial vehicle.

11-605.q: When a vehicle parks alongside the road edge (curb) on a road where parking is permitted:
11-605.a: The distance between the wheels nearest the curb and the curb must be no less than 40 cm.
11-605.b: There is no distance requirement for the curb; the requirement exists only when there is a sidewalk.
11-605.c: The distance between the wheels nearest the curb and the curb must not exceed 40 cm.
11-605.d: When there is a curb, you must park on the road shoulder.

11-606.q: Must the driver have the driving license in their possession while driving?
11-606.a: The driver must have the driving license in their possession while driving.
11-606.b: There is no need for that when traveling inside the cities.
11-606.c: This duty applies only to drivers of public vehicles.
11-606.d: No, because the police can find out by computer whether they have a license or not.

11-607.q: Is parking permitted on the road shoulder of a non-urban road?
11-607.a: Parking a vehicle on the road shoulder is prohibited because it is intended for pedestrians.
11-607.b: It is permitted for picking up or dropping off passengers, except on an expressway.
11-607.c: It is permitted only for public vehicles.
11-607.d: It is permitted, because the road shoulder is intended for parking vehicles without any restriction or condition.

11-608.q: When driving on a steep descent:
11-608.a: We must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us only if the road is wet.
11-608.b: We must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us in all cases.
11-608.c: We must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us only if the road is two-way.
11-608.d: We must keep a smaller distance from the vehicle in front of us because we are driving slowly.

11-609.q: The warning triangle is placed behind the vehicle in the following cases:
11-609.a: When the vehicle is parked during the night hours.
11-609.b: When the vehicle develops a fault and stops for any repair to be carried out.
11-609.c: At every stop of the vehicle.
11-609.d: When the vehicle stops on bends only.

11-610.q: To prevent a collision with the vehicle traveling in front of you:
11-610.a: You make eye contact with the driver of the vehicle in front of you.
11-610.b: You signal to the driver in front of you not to stop suddenly.
11-610.c: You keep a sufficient distance between your vehicle and the vehicle traveling in front of you.
11-610.d: You use the horn intermittently to alert the driver that you are traveling behind them.

11-611.q: If the lane you are traveling in is closed, you must give right of way to the vehicles in the other lanes:
11-611.b: Only if there is a continuous dividing line dividing the road.
11-611.c: Only if you are driving a slow vehicle.

11-612.q: Must right of way always be given to emergency vehicles?
11-612.a: Right of way must be given to emergency vehicles only if it is an ambulance.
11-612.b: Right of way must be given to emergency vehicles only if it is a police vehicle.
11-612.c: Right of way must be given to emergency vehicles on narrow roads only.

11-613.q: A left turn from a one-way road into a one-way road is made:
11-613.a: From the far left into the far right.
11-613.b: From the far left into the far left.
11-613.c: From the far right into the far left.
11-613.d: From the center of the road into the far left.

11-614.q: A U-turn to travel in the opposite direction is permitted:
11-614.a: If it does not cause an obstruction to traffic.
11-614.b: If it does not cause danger to road users.
11-614.c: If there is no sign prohibiting it.

11-615.a: Within 6 m of a railway crossing.
11-615.b: Within 20 m of a railway crossing.
11-615.c: Within 15 m of a railway crossing.
11-615.d: Within 5 m of a railway crossing.

11-617.q: Who is a road user in the eyes of the traffic law?
11-617.a: Whoever crosses the road from one side to the other.
11-617.c: Only whoever walks on the road.
11-617.d: Everyone who uses the road for traveling, walking or standing.

11-618.q: An area on the road marked with slanted white lines in the form of a traffic island means:
11-618.a: The main direction of traffic passes through this place.
11-618.b: An area where driving and parking of vehicles are prohibited.

11-619.q: When there is a 'Stop' sign at the intersection, you must stop:
11-619.a: At a place from which we can see the traffic.
11-619.c: Only when a vehicle approaches from the right.

11-620.q: You are driving on a long and steep downhill road; you must:
11-620.a: Drive faster and quickly clear the descending road.
11-620.b: Use neutral gear to take advantage of the descent.
11-620.c: Slow down until the end of the descent.
11-620.d: Drive at normal speed as on a level road.

11-621.q: The driver of the vehicle being overtaken must:
11-621.a: Not increase their speed, and move as far to the right as possible.
11-621.b: Use the horn to warn the overtaking driver.
11-621.d: Increase speed to get out of the danger zone.

11-622.q: To avoid colliding with the vehicle traveling in front of you when it stops suddenly, you must:
11-622.a: Try to overtake the vehicle in front of you so it ends up behind you.
11-622.b: Keep your foot on the brake pedal at all times.
11-622.c: Stop as soon as you see the brake lights.

11-623.q: When driving behind a vehicle on a rainy day, you must:
11-623.a: Keep a greater distance than the usual distance.
11-623.b: Keep a smaller distance than usual so you do not skid.
11-623.c: Leave a normal distance between you and the vehicle in front of you.
11-623.d: Get very close to the vehicle in front of you to avoid the mud.

11-624.q: Before entering a bend and while driving through it:
11-624.a: Speed must be increased to ensure an easy turn.
11-624.b: We must drive in the middle of the road.
11-624.c: Speed must be increased when entering the bend.
11-624.d: We must drive at a slow speed.

11-625.q: When driving behind another vehicle, you must keep a distance:
11-625.a: That does not exceed the length of one vehicle.
11-625.b: Sufficient to stop in order to prevent an accident.
11-625.c: Sufficient to see the license plate of the vehicle in front of us.
11-625.d: Sufficient for a quick start so we do not obstruct traffic.

11-626.q: When your vehicle is parked on an uphill road with a curb, you use the parking brake (handbrake):
11-626.a: And use first gear and point the wheels toward the center of the road.
11-626.b: And use reverse gear and point the wheels toward the edge of the curb.
11-626.c: And use reverse gear and point the wheels toward the center of the road.
11-626.d: And use first gear and point the wheels toward the edge of the curb.

11-627.q: When we drive at a higher speed:
11-627.a: The driving speed does not affect the distance we must keep.
11-627.b: We must keep a very small distance from the vehicle in front of us.
11-627.c: We must keep a greater distance when there is a general danger sign.
11-627.d: We must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us.

11-628.q: When parking the vehicle on an uphill road:
11-628.c: You must use a high gear.

11-629.q: When the driver approaches a pedestrian crossing:
11-629.a: They allow pedestrians to cross the road and stop when necessary.
11-629.b: They must not stop before the pedestrian crossing.
11-629.c: They stop their vehicle only if another vehicle has stopped before the crossing.
11-629.d: They stop driving in every case, even when there are no pedestrians.

12-630.a: Not permitted for certain types of vehicles.
12-630.c: Not permitted except in case of necessity.
12-630.d: Permitted only if there is a person guiding.

12-631.q: We must keep the distance between us and the vehicle in front of us, taking into account:

12-632.q: On a road with more than two lanes:
12-632.a: You must drive in the right lane, except when overtaking.
12-632.b: You must drive in the right lane unless the road is outside the cities.
12-632.c: Driving in the left lane is permitted for private cars, and the right lane for trucks.
12-632.d: You must drive in the right lane only when turning right.

12-633.q: A vehicle may overtake a motorcycle:
12-633.a: Only if there are two lanes in the same direction of travel.
12-633.b: When the road is not clear.
12-633.d: Only if the overtaking is done in the same lane.

12-634.q: On an uphill road, overtaking is prohibited:
12-634.a: Only if the sign prohibiting overtaking a motor vehicle traveling on more than two wheels is placed.
12-634.b: If the field of vision is limited.
12-634.d: Only if the sign meaning a general danger ahead is placed.

12-635.q: When passing a bus stopped at the station:
12-635.a: Do not increase your speed and use the horn.
12-635.b: You must slow down if doing so does not obstruct traffic.
12-635.c: You must slow down even if doing so obstructs traffic.
12-635.d: Stop until the bus leaves the station.

12-636.q: When driving alongside a bus stopped at the station, you must slow down:
12-636.a: Only if the bus is carrying children.
12-636.c: Only if the bus is full of passengers.
12-636.d: In all cases, when doing so does not obstruct traffic.

12-637.q: If the lane you are traveling in is closed, you must give right of way to the vehicles in the other lanes:
12-637.a: Only if there is a continuous dividing line dividing the road.
12-637.c: Only if you are driving a slow vehicle.

12-638.q: If the right headlight of the vehicle burns out while you are driving at night:
12-638.a: You may continue driving to the nearest garage.
12-638.b: Continuing to drive with one light is prohibited.
12-638.c: You may continue driving using the four-way flashers (hazard lights).
12-638.d: You may continue driving only behind another vehicle.

12-639.q: On a road section divided by a broken dividing line:
12-639.a: Overtaking is permitted in all cases.
12-639.b: Overtaking is permitted if there is a continuous dividing line to the right of the broken line.
12-639.c: Overtaking is permitted only if the road is not two-way.
12-639.d: Overtaking is permitted if the road is clear.

12-640.q: There are two lanes in your direction of travel; in which one do you drive?
12-640.a: The left lane may be used if the road is one-way.
12-640.b: In the right lane, except when overtaking.
12-640.c: The left lane may be used by fast vehicles only.

12-641.q: How do you act when driving on a narrow road and a vehicle comes toward you from ahead?
12-641.a: You must slow down and drive at the far right of the road, even going onto the road shoulder.
12-641.b: You must stop on the road shoulder.
12-641.c: You must only slow down and continue driving in the same lane.
12-641.d: You must not increase your speed and continue driving.

12-642.q: Is the red light in the traffic signal binding on the driver?
12-642.a: That depends on the traffic situation.

12-643.q: Is driving on a median permitted?
12-643.a: It is permitted unless there is a sign prohibiting it.
12-643.c: It is permitted if the median is paved.
12-643.d: Yes, it is permitted, but stopping on it is not.

12-644.q: The law requires driving on the far right side of the road:
12-644.a: In all cases, except when turning right.
12-644.b: On roads inside the cities only.
12-644.d: In all cases, except when overtaking.

12-645.q: When you want to overtake a vehicle, you must signal:
12-645.a: Only while passing the vehicle you are overtaking.
12-645.b: Before moving into the left lane and while overtaking it.
12-645.c: Before moving into the left lane.
12-645.d: Only if you see a vehicle coming from behind you.

12-646.q: When you are stopped before the traffic light and want to turn right:
12-646.a: There is no need to signal if there are no vehicles behind you.
12-646.b: You must signal with the turn signal (indicator).
12-646.c: There is no need to signal if there is an arrow to the right above the traffic light.
12-646.d: There is no need to signal when turning according to the traffic light.

12-647.q: When there is an obstacle such as a pole or an island on a two-way road:
12-647.a: The obstacle may be passed on its right or on its left, unless there is a sign indicating otherwise.
12-647.b: The obstacle must be passed on the right side, unless there is a sign requiring passing on its left.
12-647.c: The obstacle may be passed on its left side if there is no traffic from the opposite direction.
12-647.d: Any obstacle is always passed on its right side.

12-648.a: Right of way belongs to the ascending vehicles in all cases.
12-648.b: Right of way belongs to the ascending vehicles if it is not possible for two vehicles to pass.
12-648.c: Right of way belongs to commercial vehicles whose permissible gross weight exceeds 4,000 kg.
12-648.d: Right of way belongs to heavy vehicles.

12-649.q: When a vehicle is parked on an uphill road alongside the sidewalk:
12-649.c: The front wheels must be pointed toward the center of the road.
12-649.d: You must use the parking brake (handbrake).

12-650.q: If the vehicle in front of you signals that it wants to turn left:
12-650.a: You must wait for the vehicle until it completes its turn.
12-650.b: Overtaking this vehicle on its right side is permitted.
12-650.c: Passing this vehicle on its right side is permitted.
12-650.d: Overtaking the vehicle is permitted after alerting its driver with the horn.

12-651.q: Must the driver use the turn signal (indicator) when intending to move their vehicle off?
12-651.a: Yes, on a one-way road only.
12-651.b: Yes, only if they intend to change their lane of travel.
12-651.d: Yes, on roads inside the cities only.

12-652.q: You must keep a reasonable distance between you and the vehicle traveling in front of you:
12-652.b: On a steep descent, if there is a sign indicating it.

12-653.q: When driving behind a vehicle and increasing speed:
12-653.a: You must keep the same distance between you.
12-653.b: You must overtake it if the road is not clear.
12-653.c: You must keep a smaller distance between you and it.
12-653.d: You must keep a greater distance between you and it.

12-654.q: What distance do you keep between you and the vehicle traveling in front of you on a road inside the cities?
12-654.a: A distance sufficient to stop the vehicle at any time to prevent an accident.
12-654.d: A distance sufficient for a vehicle to enter it.

12-655.q: What distance do you keep between you and the vehicle traveling in front of you in a convoy outside the cities?
12-655.b: A distance that lets you see the rear bumper of the vehicle in front of you.
12-655.c: A distance sufficient for the field of vision needed for overtaking.
12-655.d: A distance sufficient for another vehicle to enter it.

12-656.q: You are driving with the high beam at night and suddenly a vehicle comes from ahead; when do you dip the lights?
12-656.a: From a distance of 100 meters from the oncoming vehicle.
12-656.b: From a distance of 50 meters from the oncoming vehicle.
12-656.c: Immediately upon seeing the oncoming vehicle.
12-656.d: When the other driver dips the lights in their vehicle.

12-657.q: An island-shaped area marked with slanted white lines on the road surface indicates:
12-657.a: An area on the road where stopping is permitted.
12-657.b: An area on the road where driving is not permitted.
12-657.c: An area indicating the direction of traffic.

12-658.q: Is parking beside the sidewalk at an angle permitted?
12-658.a: It is permitted if there is special marking on the road surface.
12-658.b: Parking in this manner is not permitted.
12-658.c: It is permitted for motorcycles only.
12-658.d: It is permitted on wide roads only.

12-659.q: When you are driving a vehicle and an emergency vehicle comes along the road you are traveling on:
12-659.a: Give right of way to the emergency vehicle only if there is a single lane in its path.
12-659.b: Slow down only if the emergency vehicle is in your lane.
12-659.c: Slow down, drive on the right of the road, and give right of way to the emergency vehicle.
12-659.d: Slow down, stop on the right, and give right of way to the emergency vehicle.

13-660.q: When must you slow down or stop the vehicle even though you are driving within the legal speed?
13-660.b: When approaching a soldier transport station.
13-660.d: When passing a vehicle stopped on the road shoulder.

13-661.q: When must you slow down or stop the vehicle even though you are driving within the permitted speed?
13-661.b: If the tires are worn out.
13-661.c: On a steep and long descent.

13-662.q: When must you slow down or stop the vehicle?
13-662.a: Near a group of children or near a crowd.
13-662.b: On a steep and long ascent.
13-662.c: When passing a soldier transport station.

13-663.q: Passing on the right is permitted:
13-663.a: When the vehicle in front of us wants to turn left, has signaled to that effect, and the width of the road is sufficient.
13-663.b: The law prohibits overtaking on the right in all cases.
13-663.c: When there is a sufficient distance to the right of the vehicle we want to overtake.
13-663.d: When the width of the road allows it.

13-664.q: Near schools and playgrounds, the danger to pedestrians or children is greater; therefore, when approaching these places:
13-664.a: We drive in the opposite lane to stay as far as possible from the playgrounds.
13-664.b: We slow down and drive with great caution.
13-664.c: We continue driving normally, provided we pay attention to the pedestrian crossing.
13-664.d: We use the horn the whole time to warn the children against jumping into the street.

13-665.q: At an intersection, right of way must be given to the vehicle coming from the right:
13-665.a: Only if the 'Give right of way' sign is in your lane of travel.
13-665.b: Only if the vehicle coming from the right is an emergency vehicle.
13-665.c: Only if there is a stop sign in the lane you are traveling in.
13-665.d: In all cases, when there is no traffic sign requiring giving right of way.

13-666.q: When you want to turn left at an intersection, you must give right of way to the vehicle coming from the opposite direction:
13-666.a: Only if it is turning left.
13-666.b: Only if it is going straight ahead.
13-666.c: Only if it is turning right.

13-667.q: You are driving on a narrow two-way road clear of traffic; a continuous dividing line separates the two lanes, and a cart is traveling in front of you:
13-667.a: You overtake the cart properly if the road ahead of you is clear.
13-667.b: You try to pass the cart on the right by going down onto the road shoulder.
13-667.c: You continue at a slow speed behind the cart; overtaking it is prohibited.
13-667.d: You overtake the cart so as not to obstruct traffic.

13-668.q: When approaching an intersection not equipped with signs, you must:
13-668.a: Slow down, and give right of way to vehicles coming from the right and near the intersection.
13-668.c: Increase your speed slightly to make sure you do not obstruct traffic.
13-668.d: Stop completely, to give right of way to all crossing vehicles.

13-669.q: You are driving on a one-way road and want to turn left into a two-way road; you must turn:
13-669.a: From the right side of the road into the left lane of the intended road.
13-669.b: From the left side of the road into the left lane of the intended road.
13-669.c: From the left side of the road, in a wide arc, to the far right of the intended road.
13-669.d: From the right side of the road into the right lane of the intended road.

13-670.q: On a road with two or more lanes:
13-670.a: Driving in either lane is permitted according to the driver's preference.
13-670.b: You must drive in the right lane, except when overtaking.
13-670.c: You must drive in the left lane, because the right one is intended for trucks.
13-670.d: You must drive in the right lane only when driving at a speed of no more than 30 km/h.

13-671.q: On a downhill road, you must give right of way to the ascending vehicles:
13-671.a: Only if there is the sign indicating a dangerous descent.
13-671.b: Only if the weight of the ascending vehicle exceeds 4 tons.
13-671.d: If the width of the road is not sufficient for two vehicles to pass.

13-672.q: When a vehicle is stopped to fulfill its duty before a railway crossing:
13-672.a: Overtaking is permitted if the crossing is equipped with a barrier.
13-672.b: Overtaking is permitted if there is a continuous white dividing line.
13-672.d: Overtaking it is permitted only if the width of the road exceeds 6 meters.

13-673.q: When you want to overtake a motorcycle:
13-673.a: Overtaking is permitted only if there are three lanes in the direction of travel.
13-673.b: Overtaking is permitted only if there are two lanes in the direction of travel.
13-673.c: Overtaking is permitted only on a non-urban road.
13-673.d: Overtaking is permitted only when the road is clear.

13-674.q: Overtaking before a crest on an uphill road is prohibited:
13-674.a: Only if there is the sign prohibiting overtaking.
13-674.b: Because the field of vision is limited.
13-674.c: Only if there is the sign meaning a general danger.
13-674.d: Only if the weight of the vehicle exceeds 4 tons.

13-675.q: Inside an intersection with one lane for each direction of travel:
13-675.a: Overtaking is possible only if there is the sign ending the overtaking prohibition.
13-675.c: Overtaking is possible, but only on a non-urban road.
13-675.d: Overtaking is possible if there is one lane for each direction of travel.

13-676.q: When the barrier is open at a railway crossing, you must:
13-676.a: Slow down, make sure there is no danger, then cross the crossing.
13-676.b: Stop 6 meters before the railway line.
13-676.c: Stop before the front wheels touch the railway line to check the road.
13-676.d: Stop very close to the line to make sure the road is clear.

13-677.a: There is no other way to prevent a road accident.
13-677.d: The driver wants to return to the place they came from.

13-678.a: Overtaking a private car is permitted, and overtaking a public vehicle is prohibited.
13-678.b: Overtaking is prohibited unless there are two lanes in the same direction of travel.
13-678.c: A private car is permitted to overtake, but that is prohibited for a public vehicle.
13-678.d: Overtaking a vehicle is permitted, except where there is a traffic sign prohibiting it.

13-679.q: When you reach an intersection without traffic signs and another vehicle approaches the intersection from the right:
13-679.a: Right of way must be given to the vehicle coming from the right.
13-679.b: Right of way belongs to the vehicle coming from the right only if it is a truck.
13-679.c: You have right of way because you arrived first.
13-679.d: Right of way belongs only to fast vehicles.

13-680.q: When exiting a fuel station onto the public road:
13-680.a: You must give right of way to the heavy vehicle on the road only.
13-680.b: You must give right of way to all vehicles on the road.
13-680.c: You must give right of way to the vehicle coming from the right only.
13-680.d: You must give right of way to the vehicle on the road only when turning left.

13-681.q: Before a pedestrian crossing, overtaking is prohibited:
13-681.b: In all cases on a non-urban road only.
13-681.c: Only if there is the sign warning of approaching a pedestrian crossing.
13-681.d: Only on a non-urban road with two lanes.

13-682.q: On an urban road where there is no speed limit sign, the permitted speed for a private car is:
13-682.a: 40 km/h on a side road, and no more than 60 km/h on a main road.
13-682.b: 50 km/h, plus an additional 10 km/h for each extra lane.
13-682.c: 60 km/h on a one-way road only.

13-683.q: On a two-way road comprising three lanes, you must drive:
13-683.a: In the middle or left lane.
13-683.b: In the middle lane except when stopping.
13-683.c: In the right lane, except when overtaking or turning left.
13-683.d: In the middle lane, and in the left one when turning right.

13-684.q: When there is a traffic island in front of you without a traffic sign, you must always pass it:
13-684.a: On the side where the road is wider.
13-684.b: On the right side, unless a traffic sign indicates otherwise.
13-684.c: From the place where traffic is congested.

13-685.q: When you are driving a private car on a road outside the cities:
13-685.a: Driving faster than 90 km/h is prohibited, except on the main road.
13-685.b: Driving faster than 80 km/h is prohibited, except for professional drivers.
13-685.c: Driving faster than 80 km/h is prohibited on a single-lane road.
13-685.d: Driving faster than 80 km/h is prohibited.

13-686.q: You are driving on a two-way road with one lane in each direction and a continuous dividing line:
13-686.a: Stopping and parking are absolutely prohibited.
13-686.b: Stopping and parking are permitted only alongside the sidewalk.
13-686.c: There is nothing preventing stopping and parking, unless a traffic sign prohibits it.
13-686.d: Parking is permitted for buses only.

13-687.q: You are driving at night on a non-urban road using the high beams, and a vehicle approaches from the opposite direction; when must you dip the lights so as not to dazzle its driver?
13-687.a: When the oncoming vehicle drives with dipped lights.
13-687.b: Immediately, when you see the vehicle coming from the opposite direction.
13-687.c: When your vehicle is 50 meters from the oncoming vehicle.
13-687.d: When the oncoming vehicle gives you a signal to do so.

13-688.q: The vehicle in front of you wants to turn left and has signaled to that effect:
13-688.a: Overtaking it on the right is permitted only at a junction equipped with a traffic light.
13-688.b: Overtaking on the right is prohibited in all cases.
13-688.c: You are permitted to overtake it on the right in all cases.
13-688.d: Passing it on the right is permitted, with caution, provided the width of the road is sufficient.

13-689.q: In a dangerous situation - children on the road, a pedestrian crossing - you must:
13-689.b: Use the horn until you pass the place.
13-689.d: Speed up to get past the danger quickly.

14-690.q: How do you act when we notice there is an animal on the road?
14-690.b: Pay no attention to the matter at all.

14-691.q: When approaching a narrow bridge, you must:
14-691.a: Cross the bridge at maximum speed to allow the vehicle coming from the opposite direction to cross it.
14-691.b: Stop before the bridge for caution, and then cross it quickly and safely.
14-691.c: Slow down when approaching the bridge and while crossing it.
14-691.d: Slow down when approaching the bridge and increase your speed while crossing it.

14-692.q: Right of way must be given to emergency vehicles:
14-692.b: Only if they are coming from the right.
14-692.c: Only if the road has one lane in each direction.

14-693.q: A 'Stop' traffic sign placed on the right side of the intersection, on a one-way road with two lanes:
14-693.a: You must stop only if you are in the right lane.
14-693.b: You must stop only if you are in the left lane.
14-693.c: You must stop in all cases.
14-693.d: You must stop if you want to turn left.

14-694.q: We must signal when attempting to deviate or turn:
14-694.a: From a reasonable distance before attempting to deviate or turn.
14-694.b: There is no need to signal when attempting to deviate.
14-694.c: A signal must be given five meters before changing lanes.
14-694.d: First change lanes, and then give the signal.

14-695.q: The driver must give a signal to other drivers:
14-695.a: When the driver wants to continue driving straight ahead.
14-695.b: When the driver wants to deviate from their lane of travel.
14-695.c: When the vehicle develops a fault on a non-urban road.
14-695.d: When the driver reaches a descent.

14-696.q: When turning left into a one-way road that has a median, you must enter:
14-696.a: Into the middle of the intended road.
14-696.b: According to the condition of the road and the type of vehicle.
14-696.c: Into the right side of the intended road.
14-696.d: Into the left side of the intended road.

14-697.q: On a narrow road that is not wide enough for two vehicles to pass:
14-697.a: Right of way belongs to the vehicle carrying more than 8 passengers.
14-697.b: The two oncoming vehicles must move to the far right, even going down onto the road shoulder.
14-697.c: Right of way belongs to the fast vehicle.
14-697.d: Right of way belongs to the vehicle that arrives first.

14-698.q: When there are lanes marked on the road surface for going straight, right or left:
14-698.a: Moving quickly and immediately from one lane to another is permitted.
14-698.b: Moving is permitted after taking all precautionary measures.
14-698.c: Moving is permitted within the limits of the intersection.
14-698.d: Moving from one lane to another is prohibited.

14-699.q: When the road is divided by a continuous dividing line:
14-699.a: Crossing the line is permitted to overtake an animal-drawn vehicle.
14-699.c: Crossing the line is permitted if there is a vehicle stopped on the side of the road.
14-699.d: Crossing the dividing line is permitted if there is a broken line to its right.

14-700.q: You are approaching an intersection without traffic signs and want to turn left; a vehicle approaches the intersection from your right:
14-700.a: The vehicle that wants to turn left always has right of way.
14-700.c: Slow down, and even stop when necessary, to give right of way to the vehicle coming from the right.
14-700.d: Stop only if there is the 'Stop' traffic sign.

14-701.q: You are approaching an intersection without signs and want to turn left; a vehicle approaches the intersection from ahead:
14-701.a: Slow down, and even stop when necessary, to give right of way to the vehicle coming from the opposite direction.
14-701.b: The vehicle turning left has right of way in all cases.
14-701.c: Stop only if there is the 'Stop' traffic sign.

14-702.q: When approaching the intersection and wanting to turn right, another vehicle approaches the intersection from ahead:
14-702.a: Stop and give right of way to the vehicle coming from the opposite direction.
14-702.c: Increase your speed; you have right of way.
14-702.d: Slow down and continue driving with caution.

14-703.q: When approaching an intersection without signs and wanting to go straight through it, a vehicle approaches the intersection in your direction:
14-703.b: Continue driving and cross the intersection quickly.
14-703.c: Slow down, and even stop when necessary.
14-703.d: Stop and give the vehicle right of way.

14-704.q: While you are approaching a roundabout without traffic signs, a vehicle inside the roundabout comes from your left:
14-704.b: Slow down, and even stop when necessary, to give right of way.
14-704.c: You have right of way over the vehicles inside the roundabout.
14-704.d: Increase speed to get out of the roundabout.

14-705.q: When a vehicle in front of you signals that it wants to turn left:
14-705.a: The vehicle may be passed on the right side.
14-705.b: Overtaking is prohibited from any side.
14-705.c: Overtaking is permitted only from the left side.
14-705.d: Overtaking the vehicle on the right side is prohibited.

14-706.q: When you reach a railway crossing with a barrier and the barrier comes down, where do you stop your vehicle?
14-706.a: As close as possible to the barrier.
14-706.b: Immediately after the barrier, on the side of the tracks.
14-706.c: On the right side of the barrier.
14-706.d: As far away from the barrier as possible.

14-707.q: Parking is prohibited near a fire hydrant:

14-708.q: When another vehicle tries to overtake you:
14-708.a: Drive at the far right and do not increase your speed, so the vehicle can overtake you.
14-708.b: Drive at the far right and increase your speed.
14-708.c: Drive at normal speed on the left of the lane.
14-708.d: Drive in any lane you choose and at the speed you see fit.

14-709.q: When there is a continuous dividing line with a broken white line to its left, where do we drive and how must we act?
14-709.a: We drive to the right of the continuous dividing line and do not cross it, but traffic from the opposite direction is permitted to cross it.
14-709.b: Only the ascending vehicle drives to the right of the continuous dividing line.
14-709.c: We drive to the left of the continuous dividing line.
14-709.d: If the road is wide, we drive either on its left or on its right.

14-710.q: A driving license holder may drive:
14-710.a: The types of vehicles mentioned in the traffic regulations.
14-710.b: The types of vehicles recorded in the vehicle license.
14-710.c: The types of vehicles recorded in the insurance certificate.
14-710.d: The types of vehicles recorded in their driving license.

14-711.q: When driving on a rainy day behind a vehicle:
14-711.a: You leave a normal distance between you and the vehicle in front of you.
14-711.b: You keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of you.
14-711.c: You keep the braking distance between you and the vehicle in front of you.
14-711.d: You keep a smaller distance from the vehicle in front of you for fear of skidding.

14-712.q: When your vehicle is parked on an uphill road with a curb, you use the parking brake (handbrake):
14-712.a: And use first gear and point the front wheels toward the middle of the road.
14-712.b: And use reverse gear and point the front wheels toward the edge of the curb.
14-712.c: And use first gear and point the front wheels toward the edge of the curb.
14-712.d: And use reverse gear and point the front wheels toward the middle of the road.

14-713.q: On a very steep road we must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us:
14-713.b: Only if the road is wet.
14-713.d: Only if the vehicle in front of you is driving at excessive speed.

14-714.q: May a driver drive a vehicle while under the influence of alcoholic drinks?
14-714.a: It is permitted if there is no other way to drive the vehicle.
14-714.c: It is permitted only by a doctor's order.

14-715.q: May a driver drive a vehicle under the influence of sedative drugs?
14-715.b: Yes; sedative drugs have nothing to do with driving a vehicle.
14-715.c: It is permitted if an hour has passed since taking these drugs.
14-715.d: It is permitted only by order of the treating doctor.

14-716.q: Use of the road must be in a manner that:
14-716.a: Does not obstruct traffic and does not expose anyone to danger.
14-716.c: Allows reaching the destination by the shortest route and quickly.

14-717.q: When may the high (road) beam be used in the vehicle?
14-717.b: On roads outside the cities, or when circumstances allow it.
14-717.d: On roads outside the cities and when driving in a convoy.

14-718.q: When is it permitted to signal by switching the light from low to high intermittently (flashing) at night?
14-718.a: When you signal to the driver of the vehicle in front of you that you want to overtake them.
14-718.b: The law does not permit signaling in the mentioned manner.
14-718.c: When you signal to the driver of an oncoming vehicle that is shining the high beam.
14-718.d: When passing through an intersection and wanting to go straight ahead.

14-719.b: When the vehicle develops a sudden fault.
14-719.c: When one of the wheels gets a puncture.

15-720.q: May an examiner holding an examiner's ID from the licensing authority inspect a vehicle and take it off the road?
15-720.a: That is permitted only for a police officer with the rank of first lieutenant or above.
15-720.c: The examiner may do so if they present an order from the main licensing authority.

15-721.q: When hitting a vehicle parked on the road whose owner is not present:
15-721.a: The driver only has to inform the insurance agent immediately.
15-721.b: The driver must leave a written notice on the vehicle that was hit and inform the police.
15-721.c: The driver must inform the licensing authority.
15-721.d: The driver must inform the nearest police station only.

15-722.q: May the licensing authority withdraw a driver's license for a certain period or forever?
