15-722.a: No, this power belongs to the court only.
15-722.b: No, this power belongs to the police officer.
15-722.d: Yes, only after a road accident has occurred.

15-723.q: The duty to slow down means:
15-723.a: Reducing the vehicle's speed to the point of being able to stop it to prevent any danger.
15-723.c: Reducing the speed to a reasonable speed, without any need to stop.

15-724.q: The duty to act with caution on the road falls upon:
15-724.a: Drivers of public vehicles and heavy trucks only.

15-725.q: The duty to know the traffic laws and act according to them falls upon:
15-725.a: Every driver of a motor vehicle only.
15-725.d: Only drivers who have passed courses.

15-726.q: May a police officer in official uniform stop a vehicle and inspect it even though its license is valid?
15-726.a: A police officer may inspect the vehicle's brakes and lights only.
15-726.b: That is not permitted if the vehicle has passed the annual inspection.
15-726.c: A police officer holding an examiner's ID may inspect the vehicle's fitness at any time.
15-726.d: A police officer with the rank of first lieutenant or above may inspect the vehicle's fitness at any time.

15-727.q: When you park the vehicle on a downhill road with a sidewalk and use the parking brake (handbrake):
15-727.a: You point the rear wheels toward the sidewalk and use neutral gear.
15-727.b: You point the front wheels toward the sidewalk only.
15-727.c: You point the front wheels toward the sidewalk and use reverse gear.
15-727.d: You point the front wheels toward the sidewalk and use first gear.

15-728.q: Upon any change in the driver's health condition:
15-728.a: They must inform the traffic department only in the case of a change in visual acuity.
15-728.b: They must inform the traffic department only if they are a party to a road accident.
15-728.c: They must inform the traffic department only if the insurance company asks them to.
15-728.d: They must inform the traffic department.

15-729.q: On a very steep road we must keep a greater distance from the vehicle in front of us:
15-729.c: Only in rainy weather and when there is a danger of skidding.
15-729.d: Only when there is a 'dangerous descent' traffic sign.

15-730.q: A police officer in official uniform directs you to enter, with your vehicle, a road with a 'No entry' traffic sign; what do you do?
15-730.a: You must obey the orders of a police officer in official uniform in all cases.
15-730.b: You must not obey the orders of a police officer if their order leads to a traffic violation.
15-730.c: Entering a road with a 'No entry' traffic sign is prohibited in all cases.
15-730.d: Only the orders of a police officer (commissioned) must be obeyed.

15-731.q: When the police photograph a vehicle that committed a traffic violation, who is responsible in the eyes of the law?
15-731.a: The matter is of no importance; what matters is paying the fine.
15-731.b: The owner of the vehicle, unless they prove that the vehicle was being driven by another person.
15-731.d: The driver who was photographed and can be identified.

15-732.q: Is it within the power of the licensing authority to withdraw a driving license, or not to grant it, forever or for a certain period?
15-732.b: No, a license may not be withdrawn except after a road accident.
15-732.c: No, the power belongs only to the police officer.
15-732.d: No, the power belongs only to the courts.

15-733.q: May we go up onto the sidewalk and drive on it to pass an obstacle on the road?
15-733.a: You may go up onto the sidewalk with the right wheels only.
15-733.b: The obstacle can be passed only by going up onto the sidewalk.
15-733.c: Going up onto the sidewalk to pass the obstacle is prohibited.
15-733.d: You may go up onto the sidewalk with great caution and in first gear.

15-734.q: The duty to place the warning triangle when the vehicle develops a fault is imposed:
15-734.a: On private cars and public taxis.
15-734.d: On every motor vehicle traveling on more than two wheels.

15-735.b: In vehicles exempt from the duty to install a signaling device.
15-735.c: In addition to the signaling device, if the driver considers that the circumstances justify it.
15-735.d: When the signaling device develops a fault.

15-736.q: Two vehicles collided and only material damage occurred; the drivers of the two vehicles must:
15-736.a: Wait until the insurance company agents arrive.
15-736.b: Not move the vehicles until the police arrive.
15-736.c: Move the two vehicles away and inform the local authority to clean the road.
15-736.d: Move the two vehicles off the road and remove every hazardous material from the road.

15-737.q: The duty to know the traffic laws and act according to them falls:
15-737.c: On everyone who uses the road.

15-738.q: When the vehicle is parked on a downhill road alongside the sidewalk:
15-738.a: You must use the parking brake (handbrake).
15-738.c: You must point the front wheels toward the sidewalk.

15-739.q: A vehicle may be parked on the left side of the road:
15-739.a: If the road is one-way and a sign is placed at its entrance indicating that.
15-739.b: If the width of the road exceeds 12 meters.
15-739.c: Only if the road is one-way and its width exceeds 12 meters.
15-739.d: If there is no sign prohibiting it.

15-740.q: At an intersection, right of way belongs to the vehicle coming from the right:
15-740.a: In all cases, when there is no sign indicating otherwise.
15-740.d: Only if there is a roundabout at the intersection.

15-741.q: When driving a vehicle, responsibility for the vehicle and the passengers falls upon:
15-741.a: The vehicle's driver and its owner.

15-742.a: With the driver, if they are the owner of the vehicle.
15-742.b: With the owner of the vehicle.
15-742.c: With the person responsible for the vehicle.
15-742.d: With the driver when driving the vehicle, even if they are not its owner.

15-743.b: Overtaking is permitted for private and public vehicles only.
15-743.c: Overtaking is permitted unless there is a sign prohibiting it.

15-744.q: When you reach an intersection free of signs and there is a vehicle coming from the left:
15-744.b: Give right of way to the vehicle coming from the left.
15-744.c: You have right of way only if you want to go straight ahead.
15-744.d: Give right of way if you want to turn left.

15-745.q: What is the maximum speed for a private car on an urban road?

15-746.q: When you come out onto the road from a dirt road:
15-746.a: You must give right of way only if you want to turn left.
15-746.b: You must give right of way only if a stop sign is placed there.
15-746.c: You have right of way if you are coming from the right.
15-746.d: You must give right of way to the vehicles on the road in front of you.

15-747.q: The warning triangle must be placed behind the vehicle:
15-747.a: Only if the vehicle with the fault is a private car or a public taxi.
15-747.b: Only when parking the vehicle for more than 24 hours.
15-747.c: In every case where the vehicle stops to repair a fault in it.
15-747.d: Only in the case of parking a vehicle that developed a fault and is left unattended.

15-748.q: When you drive a private car on a road outside the cities:
15-748.a: Driving faster than 80 km/h is prohibited.
15-748.b: Driving faster than 90 km/h is prohibited.
15-748.c: Driving faster than 100 km/h is prohibited.
15-748.d: Driving faster than 110 km/h is prohibited.

15-749.q: Overtaking an overtaking vehicle is permitted:
15-749.a: Only for private cars and public taxis, on a road with two or more lanes in the same direction.
15-749.b: For a commercial vehicle whose gross weight does not exceed 4 tons, on a road with more than two lanes in the same direction.
15-749.c: For every vehicle, if the road has two or more lanes in the same direction.
15-749.d: For a vehicle whose gross weight does not exceed 4 tons, on a road with two or more lanes in the same direction.

16-750.q: When your vehicle stops at the side of the road because of a fault, the warning triangle must be placed:
16-750.a: Behind the vehicle so that it can be seen by a driver coming from behind from a distance of at least 100 meters.
16-750.b: Behind the vehicle so that it can be seen by a driver coming from behind from a distance of at least 150 meters.
16-750.c: At a distance of 100 meters behind the vehicle.
16-750.d: On the road or on the rear window of the vehicle.

16-751.q: You are driving with the high beam on a road outside the cities and a vehicle comes from ahead:
16-751.a: You must dip the lights only if the vehicle in front of you is driving with the high beam.
16-751.b: You must dip the lights only if the vehicle in front of you is driving with the low beam.
16-751.c: You must dip the lights from a distance of at least 50 meters.
16-751.d: You must dip the lights immediately upon seeing it.

16-752.q: Before the crest of a slope, overtaking is prohibited:
16-752.a: Only if the weight of your vehicle is more than 4 tons.
16-752.c: Only if there is the 'No overtaking' sign.
16-752.d: In all cases when visibility is limited.

16-753.q: Is parking a vehicle on the sidewalk permitted?
16-753.a: It is permitted for cargo vehicles only, for the purpose of loading and unloading.
16-753.b: Parking a vehicle on the sidewalk is permitted if the width of the sidewalk exceeds 3 meters.
16-753.c: Parking a vehicle on the sidewalk is not permitted unless there is a sign permitting it.
16-753.d: It is permitted when the vehicle develops a fault that prevents it from continuing.

16-754.q: What is the maximum number of passengers a private car may carry?
16-754.a: Up to seven passengers, excluding the driver.
16-754.b: Up to seven passengers, including the driver.
16-754.c: Up to four passengers, excluding the driver.
16-754.d: Up to five passengers, excluding the driver.

16-755.q: When must you signal when attempting to deviate from the lane or start moving?
16-755.a: The signal must be given from a distance and time sufficient to alert the other driver.
16-755.b: The signal must be given from a distance of 50 meters before overtaking.
16-755.c: The signal must be given only when there are vehicles behind you.
16-755.d: The signal must be given after you have changed your lane of travel.

16-756.q: May a motor vehicle be towed by a private car?
16-756.a: Towing a motor vehicle by a private car is not permitted.
16-756.b: A dual-purpose private car may tow a motor vehicle.
16-756.c: It is permitted if the weight of the towed vehicle is less than the weight of the towing vehicle.
16-756.d: It is permitted provided a tow bar is used as stipulated by law.

16-757.q: May the owner of a private car transport passengers for a fare?
16-757.a: It is permitted if the owner of the private car works for an institution or company.
16-757.c: It is permitted provided the trip is private.
16-757.d: It is permitted on lines not served by public transport.

16-758.q: When your vehicle is towed away from a place where parking is prohibited, who is responsible for this violation in the eyes of the law?
16-758.d: The owner of the vehicle or its driver.

16-759.q: Whoever holds a private car driving license in which the duty to use glasses is recorded:
16-759.a: Must use the glasses only if the visibility on the road is not clear.
16-759.b: Must use the glasses while driving.
16-759.c: Must carry spare glasses with them while driving.
16-759.d: Must use the glasses while driving at night only.

16-760.q: Who is permitted to make a U-turn to travel in the opposite direction?
16-760.a: All vehicles except long vehicles whose total length exceeds 15 meters.
16-760.b: Only the driver of a vehicle whose permissible gross weight does not exceed 4 tons.
16-760.c: The driver of a private car and a public taxi only.
16-760.d: The driver of every vehicle, provided they do not cause an obstruction or danger and when there is no sign prohibiting it.

16-761.q: Which authorities are empowered to withdraw the driving license?

16-762.q: Who is responsible for the use of seat belts by the passengers in the vehicle?
16-762.d: The owner of the vehicle and its driver.

16-763.q: The area of the road shoulder extends:
16-763.a: Up to two meters from the road edge, or from the end of the sidewalk, or to the limits of the drainage channel.
16-763.b: Up to two meters from the road edge in all cases.
16-763.c: Up to three meters from the nearest continuous white line.
16-763.d: Up to three meters from the road edge, or to the limits of the drainage channel.

16-764.q: A road that may not be entered from both ends:
16-764.a: Is not considered a road in the eyes of the law.
16-764.b: Is considered a road in the eyes of the law only for the residents of the buildings on it.
16-764.c: Is considered a road in the eyes of the law for vehicle drivers only.
16-764.d: Is considered a road in the eyes of the law.

16-765.a: A part of the road width intended for pedestrians and bicycles.
16-765.b: A part of the paved road width intended for pedestrians and animals.
16-765.c: A part of the road width intended for pedestrians.
16-765.d: An area of three meters on the right side of the road.

16-766.q: May a vehicle be repaired on the road or in a public parking place?
16-766.a: It is permitted if the repair is simple and not in front of a repair workshop.
16-766.b: It is permitted for repairing a puncture only.
16-766.c: It is permitted for a vehicle mechanic only.
16-766.d: Repairing a vehicle on the road or in a public parking place is not permitted.

16-767.a: Every road at whose entrance a sign is placed indicating entry to an urban road.
16-767.c: Every road where the speed limit is set at up to 50 km/h.
16-767.d: Every road within the municipal boundaries.

16-768.a: A non-urban road designated for motor vehicles only, at whose entrance a sign indicating that is placed.
16-768.c: A road that driving-school vehicles may not enter.
16-768.d: A road that all motorcycles may not enter.

16-769.a: A road or part of a road designated for vehicles traveling on two wheels.
16-769.c: A road or part of a road intended for pedestrians only.
16-769.d: A road or part of a road, other than the street, designated for a certain type of road user.

16-770.q: What does a two-way road mean?
16-770.a: A road divided along its length by a broken dividing line.
16-770.b: A road with at least two lanes in each direction.
16-770.c: A road at whose entrance a sign indicating that is placed.
16-770.d: A road on which travel is permitted in both directions.

16-771.q: Who is permitted to drive on the road shoulder?
16-771.a: Driving on the road shoulder is prohibited.
16-771.d: Non-motorized vehicles, tractors and slow vehicles, in order to make room for them to be overtaken.

16-772.q: In which of the following cases is stopping on the shoulder of an expressway permitted?
16-772.a: For picking up and dropping off passengers.
16-772.b: For transporting the injured, towing vehicles, emergency vehicles, public works vehicles, and when the vehicle develops a fault.
16-772.c: Only when the vehicle develops a fault that prevents it from continuing.

16-773.b: Overtaking a motor vehicle is prohibited, except vehicles traveling on two wheels.
16-773.c: Overtaking all motor vehicles is prohibited.
16-773.d: Overtaking vehicles and animals as well is prohibited.

16-774.q: Is the meeting of a road with the entrance or exit of a fuel station considered an intersection?
16-774.a: The meeting of the station exit with the road is considered an intersection.
16-774.b: The meeting of the station entrance with the road is considered an intersection.
16-774.c: Yes, it is considered an intersection.
16-774.d: No, unless a sign indicates otherwise.

16-775.q: If a police officer directs a driver to enter a road that may not be entered:
16-775.a: The driver may enter the prohibited road only by reversing.
16-775.b: The driver must obey the order only if the police officer has the rank of first lieutenant or above.
16-775.c: The driver may not enter a prohibited road even if a police officer orders it.
16-775.d: The driver must obey the police officer's order.

16-776.a: The maximum speed permitted for certain vehicles on certain roads.
16-776.b: The speed that does not exceed 90 km/h.
16-776.c: The speed that does not exceed 50 km/h.
16-776.d: The speed at which one must drive on a certain road.

16-777.q: What is the maximum permitted speed for a private car on an expressway?

16-778.q: Whoever holds a class 2 (B) driving license may drive:
16-778.a: A tractor and a private car.
16-778.b: A motorcycle and a private car.
16-778.c: A private car and a commercial vehicle whose gross weight does not exceed 4 tons.
16-778.d: A commercial vehicle up to a gross weight of 2,200 kg only.

16-779.q: What penalties can the court impose on a violating driver?
16-779.c: All the penalties mentioned in the answers are correct.

17-780.q: Is using the horn inside the cities permitted?
17-780.a: It is permitted for certain types of vehicles.
17-780.b: It is permitted, but not continuously or repeatedly.
17-780.c: It is permitted when overtaking only.
17-780.d: It is permitted to avoid an imminent danger that cannot be avoided in any other way.

17-781.q: On a non-urban road, stopping on the road shoulder is permitted:
17-781.a: For dropping off and picking up passengers, except on an expressway.
17-781.b: It is permitted for public vehicles only.
17-781.c: It is permitted for all types of vehicles.

17-782.a: From the far left into the far right.
17-782.b: From the right side into the right side of the intended road, as far as possible.
17-782.c: From the far right into the far left.
17-782.d: From as close as possible to the center of the road into the far right.

17-783.a: A vehicle that has only one rear axle.
17-783.b: A vehicle whose weight does not exceed 4,000 kg.
17-783.c: A vehicle used, or intended for use, in transporting goods.
17-783.d: A vehicle whose weight is less than 15 tons.

17-784.q: A U-turn to travel in the opposite direction is permitted:
17-784.a: When there is no sign prohibiting it.
17-784.c: In every case where doing so does not expose road users to danger.
17-784.d: In every case where doing so does not obstruct traffic.

17-785.a: Within 12 meters before a pedestrian crossing and within 12 meters after it.
17-785.b: Within 20 meters before a pedestrian crossing.
17-785.c: Within 12 meters before a pedestrian crossing.
17-785.d: Within 15 meters before a pedestrian crossing.

17-786.a: Within 15 meters of a bus stop.
17-786.b: Within 20 meters of a bus stop.
17-786.c: Within 12 meters of a bus stop.
17-786.d: Within 20 meters of a sign indicating a bus stop.

17-787.q: When parking a vehicle alongside the sidewalk, the distance between the wheels nearest the sidewalk and the sidewalk:
17-787.b: Must be no less than 40 cm.

17-788.q: Is parking a vehicle on a median permitted?
17-788.a: It is permitted only if there is a dividing line.
17-788.b: It is permitted after taking all precautionary measures.
17-788.c: Parking on a median is permitted, but driving on it is not.

17-789.a: A non-urban road reserved for motor vehicles only, with a sign placed at its entrance indicating that the road is an expressway.
17-789.b: An urban or non-urban road comprising three lanes for each direction.
17-789.c: A road everyone may drive on at a maximum speed of no more than 90 km/h.
17-789.d: There is no special sign for an expressway.

17-790.q: When turning left at the intersection from two adjacent lanes designated for turning left:
17-790.a: Overtaking at the junction is not permitted.
17-790.b: Overtaking on the right is permitted.
17-790.c: Passing on the right is permitted.

17-791.a: The area intended for pedestrians to walk.
17-791.b: The yellow line drawn on the paved road.
17-791.c: The area adjacent to the road edge, intended only for dropping off or picking up passengers.
17-791.d: The area adjacent to the edge of any road without a sidewalk, up to 3 meters or up to the edge of the drainage channel.

17-792.q: When exiting a vehicle parking place:
17-792.a: Give right of way to the vehicles on the road crossing in front of you.
17-792.b: Give right of way only if you want to turn left.
17-792.c: Give right of way to the vehicles coming from the right.
17-792.d: You have right of way if you are coming from the right.

17-793.q: Which documents must be with the driver while driving a vehicle?
17-793.a: The driving license, the ID, the vehicle license, the insurance certificate, and any other certificate required by law or regulations.
17-793.b: An ID, the insurance certificate and the driving license.
17-793.c: The driving license and the insurance.
17-793.d: An ID and the driving license, while the vehicle license and the insurance remain with the owner of the vehicle.

17-794.a: Whoever holds a valid license to drive a motor vehicle.
17-794.b: Every driver holding a class ten driving license.
17-794.c: Every driver who has reached eighteen years of age.
17-794.d: Whoever holds a valid driving license for the same type of vehicle, and an insurance certificate that covers the driver.

17-795.q: Is the sun visor necessary in a motor vehicle?
17-795.a: Yes, for all vehicles except tall vehicles such as buses and trucks.
17-795.c: Yes, for every motor vehicle that has a windshield.
17-795.d: Yes, for all types of vehicles.

17-796.q: The danger of skidding on a wet road is greater:
17-796.a: The more the tires are unfit and worn smooth.
17-796.b: There is no relation between the condition of the tires and skidding.
17-796.c: The deeper the tire treads are.
17-796.d: When the tire size is not suitable for the vehicle.

17-797.q: What is the purpose of the markings on the road surface?
17-797.a: They give the driver information about the width of the road only.
17-797.b: They help the driver when overtaking and enable them to give right of way.
17-797.c: They help organize traffic and make maximum use of the width of the road.
17-797.d: They give the driver information about the direction of the lanes only.

17-798.q: When driving at night and the vehicle coming toward you dazzles your eyes:
17-798.a: Use the road (high) beam and direct your eyes to the right side of the road.
17-798.b: Use the road (high) beam and stop on the right side, if necessary, until the vehicle passes.
17-798.c: Do not use the road (high) beam, and direct your eyes to the right side of the road.
17-798.d: Use the flashing high beam to alert the other driver.

17-799.q: Skidding of the vehicle's wheels is caused by:

17-800.q: When one of the vehicle's tires gets a puncture:
17-800.a: You replace the damaged wheel with the passengers outside the vehicle.
17-800.b: You replace the damaged wheel with the passengers inside the vehicle.
17-800.c: You replace the damaged wheel with only the children inside the vehicle.
17-800.d: You replace the damaged wheel with the passengers on the side of that wheel outside the vehicle.

17-801.q: The reaction distance is the distance the vehicle travels:
17-801.a: From the moment of pressing the brake pedal until coming to a complete stop.
17-801.b: From the moment of seeing the danger until pressing the brake pedal.
17-801.c: From the moment of lifting the foot off the accelerator pedal until coming to a complete stop.
17-801.d: From the moment of seeing the danger until coming to a complete stop.

17-802.q: What does sand on the road surface cause?
17-802.a: It causes an increase in friction between the tires and the road surface.
17-802.b: There is no effect on the vehicle when there is sand on the road surface.
17-802.c: It reduces the vehicle's skidding on the road.
17-802.d: Little friction between the tires and the road surface, and consequently the vehicle skids.

17-803.q: You are driving on an urban road at 50 km/h and a vehicle is traveling in front of you in the same direction and at the same speed:
17-803.a: You overtake it only on a one-way road with two or more lanes.
17-803.b: You overtake it only if there is no sign prohibiting overtaking.
17-803.c: You overtake it only if the road is clear.

17-804.q: The basic purpose of the traffic lights at the intersection is:
17-804.a: Organizing traffic and distributing the giving of right of way on the road.
17-804.b: Providing the police services needed to organize traffic only.
17-804.c: To set the driving speed of vehicles.
17-804.d: Photographing violating drivers with the electronic camera.

17-805.a: Only if your vehicle is newly manufactured.
17-805.b: Only on a one-way road with more than one lane.
17-805.c: Only on a road wider than 8 meters.
17-805.d: Only if the road conditions and traffic conditions allow it.

17-806.q: Before a narrow two-way bridge without a warning sign, you must:
17-806.a: Drive in the middle of the road so as not to scrape the guard walls.
17-806.b: Stop in all cases to give right of way.
17-806.c: Slow down and increase caution so as not to collide with the vehicle coming from the opposite direction.
17-806.d: Use the horn to warn the driver coming from the opposite direction.

17-807.q: The distance sufficient for the overtaking maneuver:
17-807.a: Depends on the speed of both vehicles, the overtaking vehicle and the vehicle being overtaken.
17-807.b: Depends on the speed of the overtaking vehicle only.
17-807.c: Depends on the speed of the vehicle being overtaken only.
17-807.d: Does not depend on the speed of the two vehicles, the overtaking vehicle and the vehicle being overtaken.

17-808.q: When approaching an intersection behind a vehicle whose driver is hesitant about which direction to go, may you overtake them?
17-808.b: Yes, because you overtake according to the law.
17-808.c: Yes, you are not obliged to wait because of the driver's hesitation.
17-808.d: Yes, if the intersection is equipped with a traffic light and the light is green.

17-809.q: You prepared to overtake and suddenly saw in the mirror a vehicle traveling fast that has started overtaking you:
17-809.a: You continue overtaking and signal by hand to the driver behind you.
17-809.b: You continue overtaking because you started overtaking before the vehicle behind you.
17-809.c: You continue overtaking because you have the right of way in overtaking.
17-809.d: You do not overtake; continue at your normal speed and let the vehicle behind you overtake.

18-810.q: You are driving on a road and suddenly a vehicle comes from the opposite direction in the lane you are traveling in; you must:
18-810.a: Slow down and move to the right as far as possible, even going out onto the road shoulder.
18-810.b: Use the horn to signal the driver to return to their lane.
18-810.c: Use the high beam to warn the driver coming from the opposite direction to return to their lane.
18-810.d: Move to the left, into the lane of the opposite direction.

18-811.q: If there is doubt that the vehicle traveling in front of you may swerve to the left, may you overtake it?
18-811.a: You must overtake at the highest speed possible.
18-811.b: You must use the horn to warn the driver when overtaking.
18-811.d: You must flash the lights to warn the driver you are overtaking.

18-812.q: You are driving behind a vehicle and the sun's rays dazzle your eyes; in this case:
18-812.a: Drive at a speed that suits your range of vision.
18-812.b: Direct your eyes down at the road close to your vehicle.
18-812.c: Get very close to the vehicle in front of you so it blocks the sun's rays.
18-812.d: Overtake the vehicle in front of you.

18-813.q: If you feel a puncture occur while driving the vehicle, you must:
18-813.a: Stop the vehicle using the foot brake and handbrake together.
18-813.b: Press the brakes gradually and slowly until the vehicle stops.
18-813.c: Press the brake pedal hard to stop the vehicle immediately.
18-813.d: Shift to a low gear and drive at a slow speed to the garage.

18-814.q: The distance the vehicle travels from the moment you press the brake pedal until stopping is greater:
18-814.a: If the driving speed is slow.
18-814.b: The harder we press the brake pedal.
18-814.c: There is no relation between speed and stopping.
18-814.d: The higher the driving speed is.

18-815.q: How do you drive on a wet road with holes full of water?
18-815.a: There is no relation between speed and a wet road.
18-815.b: You increase speed to cross these holes quickly.
18-815.c: You slow down and drive at a reasonable speed according to the conditions.
18-815.d: You press the brake pedal alternately.

18-816.q: When driving on a wet road:
18-816.a: There is a possibility that the vehicle will tend to skid the more the speed increases.
18-816.b: The vehicle skids unless we press the brake pedal intermittently.
18-816.c: The vehicle overturns if the speed is slow.
18-816.d: There is no effect on the vehicle if the tires are in good condition.

18-817.q: Before starting to drive, it is important to check:
18-817.a: The position of the gear lever.
18-817.b: The amount of fuel in the fuel tank.
18-817.d: The suitability of the seat for the driver.

18-818.q: What must the driver do when approaching a section of road with oil on it?
18-818.a: Turn the steering wheel right and left while driving over the oil.
18-818.b: Increase speed and not press the brake pedal.
18-818.c: Continue driving normally, because oil helps increase friction.
18-818.d: Slow down very much and try not to use the brakes.

18-819.q: When driving at night, the reaction time is:
18-819.b: The reaction time does not change in the dark.
18-819.c: Shorter, because the driver's senses are alert.
18-819.d: The reaction time depends on the fitness of the brakes.

18-820.q: When reversing and turning the steering wheel to the left:
18-820.a: The rear of the vehicle moves to the right.
18-820.b: The rear of the vehicle moves to the left.
18-820.c: The rear wheels move to the right.
18-820.d: The front of the vehicle moves to the left.

18-821.q: A vehicle has stopped in front of you inside an intersection not equipped with a traffic light and full of traffic:
18-821.a: Overtaking is possible if there is a sign indicating the end of the overtaking prohibition.
18-821.c: Overtaking is possible only on a non-urban road.

21-918.b: The amount of fuel in the fuel tank.
21-918.c: The fitness of the fuel system.
21-918.d: The quality of the fuel and its octane number.

21-919.q: The light-switching device in the vehicle enables the driver to:
21-919.a: Change the high beam to the road light.
21-919.b: Change the low beam to the passing (dipped) light.
21-919.d: Change the high beam to the low beam and vice versa.

21-920.q: The oil in the sump must be changed:
21-920.a: After a number of kilometers, according to the manufacturer's instructions.
21-920.b: When the amount of oil inside the engine is less than required.
21-920.c: According to the instructions of the licensing authority.
21-920.d: When you feel that the oil's viscosity is very high.

21-921.q: The lights warning lamp in the instrument panel lights up:
21-921.a: When we use the parking lights.
21-921.b: When we use the passing (dipped) light.
21-921.c: When we use the high beam.
21-921.d: When we use the low beam.

21-922.q: In modern vehicles there is a brake system lamp that lights up:
21-922.a: In every case when the parking brake (handbrake) is not used.
21-922.b: When the brake lining heats up.
21-922.c: In every case when the foot brake is used.
21-922.d: When there is a shortage of brake fluid.

21-2095.q: What is the electronic stability control system in the vehicle (E.S.P)?
21-2095.a: A system that prevents the possibility of a fault occurring in the vehicle's braking system.
21-2095.b: A system that prevents the possibility of a fault occurring in the vehicle's steering system.
21-2095.c: A system that corrects the driver's errors when braking, prevents skidding, and helps increase the vehicle's stability.
21-2095.d: A system that prevents the vehicle's wheels from locking during sudden braking, thereby keeping the vehicle's steering functional.

21-2096.q: Which part of the vehicle reduces the backward whipping of the head during a collision?
21-2096.a: The head restraint in the vehicle's seats.

21-2097.q: Which accessories reduce the severity of injury to passengers when a road accident occurs?
21-2097.a: Seat belts, head restraints and airbags.
21-2097.c: Rear window wipers and head restraints.

21-2098.q: To shift the gear from park (P) to a driving position (R) or (D) in a vehicle with an automatic gearbox:
21-2098.a: You must press the start button, and the engagement happens automatically.
21-2098.b: You must press the clutch pedal and engage the gear lever.
21-2098.c: You must press the brake pedal and the release button on the gear lever, and engage the gear lever according to the driving position.
21-2098.d: You must press the brake pedal, and the engagement happens automatically.

21-2099.q: Why must the parking brake (handbrake) be used only when parking?
21-2099.a: Because its lifespan is short and it must not be used up instead of the regular brake.
21-2099.b: Using the parking brake (handbrake) as an emergency brake is not permitted.
21-2099.c: Because the hand or the lever operating it is uncomfortable to use.
21-2099.d: The parking brake (handbrake) does not act on all the vehicle's wheels and its power is very weak.

21-2100.q: What is the function of the ABS system?
21-2100.a: To be used as an additional brake in time of danger.
21-2100.b: To enable control of the vehicle and the steering while braking.
21-2100.d: To prevent excessive use of the vehicle's brakes.

21-2101.q: Why is the soundness of the vehicle's bumpers important?
21-2101.a: In order to maintain a decent appearance for the vehicle.
21-2101.b: In order to prevent damage in severe accidents.
21-2101.c: In order to reduce the extent of damage to the vehicle and its passengers when an accident occurs.
21-2101.d: In order to avoid a relatively minor accident.

22-2102.q: What is the function of the ABS system?
22-2102.a: Helping with a smooth and more moderate braking on a wet street.
22-2102.b: Maintaining the ability to steer and maneuver the vehicle even during emergency braking.
22-2102.c: Shortening the lifespan of the tires.
22-2102.d: Helping with a smooth and more moderate braking on a dry street.

22-2103.q: What is the function of the electronic stability control system in the vehicle (E.S.P)?
22-2103.a: Reducing the amount of burnt gases emitted from the engine.
22-2103.c: A system that reduces the danger of the vehicle skidding.

22-2104.q: How does the electronic stability control system (E.S.P) work in dangerous driving situations?
22-2104.a: It applies the brakes on each wheel individually and controls the engine's power.
22-2104.c: It controls the engine's power only.
22-2104.d: It checks the air pressure in the tires and applies the parking brake to reduce the vehicle's speed.

22-2105.q: Which system helps maintain the vehicle's balance during skidding and danger of overturning?
22-2105.d: The vehicle's fuel consumption monitoring system.

22-2106.q: Why is installing hard, protruding accessories in the passenger cabin prohibited?
22-2106.a: In order to prevent possible damage to the vehicle's tires.
22-2106.b: In order to prevent impairing the passengers' field of vision while reversing.
22-2106.c: Because they disturb the vehicle's stability.
22-2106.d: Because such accessories, when an accident occurs, could injure the passengers and the driver.

22-2107.q: In a vehicle fitted with airbags:
22-2107.a: There is no need to install seat belts.
22-2107.b: Seat belts must be fastened only if there is a fault in the airbag system.
22-2107.c: Seat belts need not be fastened if there is a fault in the airbag system.
22-2107.d: It is very important to use the seat belts at every moment the vehicle is in motion.

22-2108.q: The production date of the vehicle's tire is:
22-2108.d: Recorded in the vehicle's insurance certificate.

22-2109.q: What could be the cause of a fast flashing light in the turn signal (indicator) lamp on the instrument panel in the driver's cabin?
22-2109.b: One of the turn signal (indicator) lamps is not working or is unfit.
22-2109.c: This phenomenon results from a fault in the instrument panel.
22-2109.d: The indicator switch is not in its correct position.

22-2110.q: Which bulb do you choose when you have to replace a bulb in the vehicle?
22-2110.a: The type of bulb is not important; what matters is that it fits the base of the previous bulb.
22-2110.b: Any bulb whose power equals the power of the original bulb.
22-2110.c: Any bulb whose color is similar to the color of the original bulb.
22-2110.d: A suitable bulb according to the instructions of the vehicle manufacturer.

22-2111.q: What are the dangers of driving a vehicle with a leak from the exhaust system of the vehicle's engine?
22-2111.b: Burnt gases entering the passenger cabin and causing dizziness.
22-2111.d: There is no danger in driving a vehicle with a leak from the exhaust system.

22-2112.q: How can the extent of air pollution from the exhaust gases emitted by the vehicle be reduced?
22-2112.a: By regular maintenance of the vehicle's engine and all its systems.
22-2112.b: By keeping the vehicle's engine and engine compartment clean.
22-2112.c: By washing the vehicle's fuel tank once a month.
22-2112.d: Pollution cannot be reduced because it is fixed in the vehicle.

22-2113.q: What is the navigation device (GPS) in the vehicle?
22-2113.a: A secondary device inside the steering system that boosts the steering.
22-2113.b: A device that provides information about the vehicle's location and helps the driver reach their destination.
22-2113.c: A device that checks the fitness of the computerized systems in the vehicle.
22-2113.d: A device that measures the vehicle's fuel consumption.

22-2114.q: Where do you place the jack when you want to change a wheel on the vehicle?
22-2114.a: The place is of no importance.
22-2114.b: At the place marked by the manufacturer, or at another safe place near the wheel we want to change.
22-2114.c: At the place most comfortable for the vehicle's driver.
22-2114.d: As close as possible to the rear part of the vehicle.

22-2115.q: Headlights that have not been calibrated (aimed):
22-2115.b: Do not affect driving or control of the vehicle.
22-2115.d: Do not light the road as required.

22-2116.q: When the auxiliary brake (handbrake) lamp lights up on the instrument panel while driving, it means:
22-2116.a: Everything is fine; this is a monitoring lamp that should stay lit as long as the handbrake is sound.
22-2116.b: The auxiliary brake is not working (the handbrake is down) and must be engaged.
22-2116.c: The auxiliary brake (handbrake) is sound and the lamp is faulty and must be replaced.
22-2116.d: The auxiliary brake (handbrake) is engaged (up) and must be released, because it is actually braking the vehicle.

22-2117.q: In a vehicle with a sound braking system that includes ABS:
22-2117.a: Pressing hard on the brake pedal makes the vehicle stop 'on the spot'.
22-2117.b: Pressing hard on the brake pedal turns off the engine immediately.
22-2117.c: Pressing hard on the brake pedal causes vibrations in the pedal and noise.
22-2117.d: Pressing hard on the brake pedal cancels the operation of the ABS.

22-2118.q: What is the working mechanism of the hybrid vehicle?
22-2118.a: A vehicle fitted with an electric motor in addition to the regular engine.
22-2118.b: A vehicle fitted, in addition to the engine, with a motor running on solar energy.
22-2118.c: A vehicle fitted with two regular engines.
22-2118.d: A vehicle fitted with two electric motors instead of the regular engine.

22-2119.q: One of the advantages of the hybrid vehicle is:
22-2119.a: Higher fuel consumption than a regular vehicle because of the second engine.
22-2119.b: Braking is harder because of the second engine.
22-2119.c: Lower fuel consumption than a regular vehicle.
22-2119.d: The tires wear out more than in a regular vehicle because of the second engine.

22-2120.q: What operations must the driver perform after parking their vehicle?
22-2120.a: Using the parking brake (handbrake), engaging the appropriate gear, turning off the engine, and removing the key from the ignition switch.
22-2120.b: Turning off the engine, placing a warning triangle, and locking the vehicle's doors.
22-2120.c: Removing the key from the ignition switch, placing a warning triangle, and using the parking brake (handbrake).
22-2120.d: Placing the parking sticker on the windshield on the left side, in a place visible to the municipal inspector's eyes.

22-2121.q: Must the speedometer be in sound condition in the vehicle?
22-2121.a: No, there is no duty for it to be in sound condition; it is enough to drive at a permitted speed.
22-2121.c: No, according to the driver's preference.
22-2121.d: Not always; only in a vehicle fitted with a tachograph.

22-2122.a: Works automatically in time of fog.
22-2122.c: Can be turned on only when the headlights are on.
22-2122.d: Must be installed in buses only.

22-2123.q: How do we secure a parked vehicle equipped with an automatic gearbox?
22-2123.a: We move the gear lever to first gear (1) and use the parking brake (handbrake).
22-2123.b: We move the gear lever to neutral (N) and use the parking brake (handbrake).
22-2123.c: We move the gear lever to reverse (R) and use the parking brake (handbrake).
22-2123.d: We use the parking brake (handbrake) and move the gear lever to the park position (P).

22-2124.q: What operations must the driver perform before moving the gear lever in order to start driving?
22-2124.a: Pressing the accelerator pedal so that this leads to moving off immediately.
22-2124.b: Releasing the handbrake in order to unlock the gear lever.
22-2124.c: Pressing the foot brake pedal in order to prevent the vehicle from moving.
22-2124.d: Lifting the foot off the clutch pedal so that the engine connects with the gearbox.

22-2125.q: In a vehicle fitted with a rear camera:
22-2125.a: Turn as far as possible from the sidewalk on the right.
22-2125.b: Keep away from the right edge of the street.
22-2125.c: Entering and exiting the intersection must be done in a wide arc toward the middle of the street.
22-2125.d: The camera reveals the blind spot formed behind the vehicle.

22-2126.q: Proper use of the seat belt:
22-2126.a: Reduces the injury of the person wearing the belt when an accident occurs.
22-2126.b: Reduces the driver's swaying in their seat and prevents an accident.
22-2126.d: Maintains an upright sitting position for the driver.

22-2127.q: What must we see in the vehicle's interior mirror?
22-2127.a: Those sitting in the rear seats.
22-2127.b: The view visible from behind and the 'blind spots' in it.
22-2127.c: The surroundings visible beyond the rear window, as much as possible.
22-2127.d: The view visible from the right and the left of the vehicle.

22-2128.q: What must we make sure of before starting the vehicle's engine?
22-2128.a: The handbrake is released, the gear lever is in first gear, and the clutch is pressed.
22-2128.b: The handbrake is engaged, the gear lever is in first gear (and in an automatic vehicle - D), and the clutch is pressed.
22-2128.c: The handbrake is released, the gear lever is in neutral (N) (and in an automatic vehicle - N), and the clutch is pressed.
22-2128.d: The foot brake is pressed, the handbrake is engaged, the gear lever is in neutral (N), and the clutch is pressed (and in an automatic vehicle - the park position (P)).

22-2129.q: What is the correct way to adjust the head restraint in the vehicle?
22-2129.a: The lower edge of the restraint must be level with the top of the driver's head.
22-2129.b: The upper edge of the restraint must be level with the top of the driver's head.
22-2129.c: Fixing it in the lowest possible position.
22-2129.d: The upper edge of the restraint must be in line with the bottom line of the driver's neck.

22-2130.q: Before starting the engine, the driver must make sure:
22-2130.a: That there is a sufficient amount of windshield washer fluid in the appropriate container.
22-2130.b: That no one is standing beside the vehicle.
22-2130.c: That the parking brake (handbrake) is released.
22-2130.d: That the parking brake (handbrake) is engaged (working).

22-2131.q: Before shifting from (P) to another gear in the automatic gearbox, the driver must:
22-2131.a: Press hard on the brake pedal.
22-2131.b: Press the brake pedal and the clutch pedal at the same time, and then engage the required gear.
22-2131.c: Press hard on the accelerator pedal.
22-2131.d: Press hard on the clutch pedal.

23-2132.q: The accessories that reduce the severity of injury to passengers when a road accident occurs are:
23-2132.b: Rear window wipers and head restraints.
23-2132.c: Seat belts, airbags and head restraints.
23-2132.d: Head restraints and the navigation device (G.P.S.).

23-2133.q: By what means is the braking signal given?
23-2133.a: By putting the hand out of the right window.
23-2133.b: By putting the hand out of the left window.
23-2133.c: By turning on the four-way flashers (hazard lights).
23-2133.d: By the brake light coming on.

23-2134.q: If the oil pressure lamp lights up while driving, you must:
23-2134.a: Continue driving, provided there is a sufficient amount in the engine's oil sump.
23-2134.b: Stop the vehicle immediately and turn off the engine.
23-2134.c: Continue driving in a low gear until the garage (but no more than half an hour of travel).
23-2134.d: Continue driving if the engine temperature gauge indicates that the engine temperature is normal.

23-2135.q: What are the first operations the driver must perform after entering the vehicle and before starting the journey?
23-2135.a: Adjusting the seat, the steering wheel, the mirrors and the head restraint.
23-2135.b: Starting and warming up the vehicle.
23-2135.c: Checking the position of the gear lever.

23-2136.q: One of the most important advantages of hybrid vehicles is:
23-2136.a: Lower air pollution and protecting the environment from pollution.
23-2136.b: Exhaust gas emission stops for the whole distance of the trip.
23-2136.c: Traveling most of the trip's distance using the gasoline engine.
23-2136.d: An increase in the vehicle's weight and a reduction in its speed.

23-2137.q: When the 'check engine' indicator appears on the vehicle's display, what does it mean?
23-2137.a: It means there is a fault inside the engine's torque converter.
23-2137.b: It means there is a fault in the vehicle's clutch.
23-2137.c: It means there is a fault inside the gearbox.
23-2137.d: It means there is a fault inside the engine.

23-2138.a: It is a fluid extracted from crude oil.
23-2138.b: It is a fluid extracted from gasoline fuel.
23-2138.c: It is a solution of urea and water (deionized water).
23-2138.d: It is a fluid extracted from diesel fuel.

23-2139.a: In vehicles with a two-stroke engine.
23-2139.b: In vehicles with a four-stroke engine.
23-2139.c: In vehicles with a gasoline engine.
23-2139.d: In vehicles running on a diesel engine only.

23-2140.q: What is the importance of diesel exhaust fluid (AdBlue) in diesel vehicles?
23-2140.b: It helps in the fuel combustion process.
23-2140.c: It reduces the emission of toxic gases from the exhaust pipe.

23-2141.q: How do you know that the diesel exhaust fluid (AdBlue) has run out?
23-2141.a: Through a special indicator on the instrument panel dedicated to that.
23-2141.b: Through a drop in the engine oil pressure.
23-2141.c: Through a drop in the vehicle's fuel level.
23-2141.d: Through a rise in the engine's temperature.

23-2142.q: When the diesel exhaust fluid (AdBlue) runs out in the vehicle, it is possible that:
23-2142.b: The vehicle's engine power decreases or it stops working (depending on the manufacturer).
23-2142.d: Nothing happens and the engine keeps working normally.

23-2143.q: Where could the fault be when the 'check engine' indicator appears?
23-2143.a: A fault in the main lighting system.
23-2143.b: A fault in the vehicle's transmission systems.
23-2143.c: In one of the engine's systems, such as the ignition system, the fuel system, the emissions system or the exhaust system.
23-2143.d: A fault in the auxiliary equipment systems installed around the engine.

23-2144.q: When the 'check engine' indicator appears on the instrument panel display flashing, this means:
23-2144.a: There is an unimportant fault in the engine and the vehicle need not be taken to the service center.
23-2144.b: There is a fault in the vehicle's battery charging system and it must be replaced.
23-2144.d: There is a major fault in the engine; the vehicle must be taken to the service center.

23-2145.q: How can we reduce the air pollution produced by our vehicle?
23-2145.a: By keeping the vehicle's engine working properly and according to the manufacturer's instructions, and keeping the air pressure in the tires correct.
23-2145.b: Changing the air flow sensor every two months.
23-2145.c: Using various additives with the vehicle's fuel.
23-2145.d: Keeping the fuel tank constantly full.

23-2146.q: How can the pollution emitted from the vehicle be reduced?
23-2146.a: Changing the oil filters every 10,000 km.
23-2146.b: Changing the air filters every 10,000 km.
23-2146.c: Changing the fuel filters every 10,000 km.
23-2146.d: By filling with the appropriate fuel and periodic maintenance of the vehicle.

23-2147.q: Driving downhill in neutral gear is:
23-2147.d: Not mentioned at all in the law, and it saves a lot of fuel.

23-2148.q: What must we see in the vehicle's exterior (side) mirrors?
23-2148.a: The rear wheel and the area in front of it.
23-2148.b: Any vehicle on the left side of the vehicle, and pedestrians as well.
23-2148.c: Any vehicle on the right of the vehicle while it is moving.
23-2148.d: The side edge of the vehicle and the road on both sides of the vehicle.

23-2149.q: What is the danger of filling the fuel tank while the engine is running?
23-2149.a: The engine's excessive heat could ignite the fuel.
23-2149.b: There is no danger in keeping the engine running while filling the vehicle with fuel.
23-2149.c: The fuel vapor igniting by a spark.
23-2149.d: An engine running while refueling increases fuel consumption.

signals.b63b: Two separate paths, for bicycles and for pedestrians only

signals.c39: The number of lanes is increasing
signals.c40: The number of lanes is decreasing

signals.d1: Broken line: road center line or lane line. Drivers must keep to the far right lane and may not cross the line with the vehicle or any part of it, except to overtake or to comply with a legal requirement
signals.d2: Parallel broken lines: lane lines on a road with three or more lanes. Drivers must keep to the far right lane and may not cross the lines with the vehicle or any part of it, except to overtake or to comply with a legal requirement
signals.d3: Double broken yellow line, used to mark a public transport lane: travel in the lane is permitted for the vehicles listed on sign B-35
signals.d4: Continuous dividing line: the driver must keep the vehicle or animal to the right of the line and may not cross it with the vehicle or any part of it
signals.d5: Dense broken line (guide line): indicates that a continuous dividing line is about to appear; move into the right lane before the continuous line begins
signals.d6: Broken line beside a continuous dividing line: the lines may be crossed if the broken line is to the right of the continuous line and the road is clear
signals.d7: Edge line: marks the road edge where there are no curb stones. Drivers must keep to the left of the line and may cross to its right only to prevent an accident or to avoid obstructing traffic
signals.d8: Stop line: where a vehicle or animal must stop (before the line and close to it) when there is a duty to stop
signals.d9: Line of rectangles: indicates a change of lane direction, for turning right or left only
signals.d10: Pedestrian crossing: if the crossing is divided into two parts, pedestrians must cross on the right-hand part
signals.d11: Direction arrows on the road surface
signals.d12: Guide lines for turning at intersections (broken lines or studs)
signals.d13: Arrows for movement through the intersection: the intersection may be crossed only from the lane marked with the arrow, in the direction of the arrow
signals.d14: Traffic islands (of various shapes) marked on the road: drivers of vehicles or animals may not drive or stop on them
signals.d15: Curb stones painted red and white: no stopping or parking on the road except to comply with a legal requirement
signals.d16: No-parking area: parking within the area marked on the road surface is prohibited
signals.d17: Stopping place for certain vehicle types where there is no bay: no vehicle may stop within the marked rectangle unless it is of the type for which the sign was placed (bus stop, public taxi, etc.), and only to pick up or drop off passengers
signals.d18: Stopping place for certain vehicle types where a bay exists: no vehicle may stop inside the bay or beside the broken line; only a vehicle of the type permitted by a sign may stop inside the bay, and only to pick up or drop off passengers
signals.d20: Curb stones painted alternately blue: parking on the road beside the curb is permitted with parking cards
signals.d21: Curb stones painted red and yellow: no stopping except for vehicle types permitted to stop by a sign (service-line bus stop, public taxi station, soldier transport stop)
signals.e1: (Red light) Stop! If a stop line is marked, stop before it; if there is none, stop before the pedestrian crossing; if neither is marked, stop beside the nearest traffic light
signals.e2: (Red light with yellow) Stop as for sign E-1 until the green light appears. Do not cross the stop line or the pedestrian crossing or enter the intersection while the red light shows with the yellow. Prepare to move when the signal changes
signals.e3: (Green light) Proceed! You may go straight ahead, right, or left, unless a guidance sign above the traffic light indicates otherwise. A flashing green light means the green is about to end and yellow will follow
signals.e5: (Yellow light) Anyone approaching the intersection must stop as for sign E-1, unless the driver cannot stop safely before the intersection; anyone who has entered the intersection must clear it immediately
signals.e6: Special traffic light for vehicles before a pedestrian crossing that has a pedestrian light, or before another obstruction on the road: (Red light) stop before the stop line, and if there is none, before the pedestrian crossing
signals.e7: Special traffic light for vehicles before a pedestrian crossing or another obstruction: (Yellow light) (a) flashing yellow: proceed attentively, observing the signs; (b) steady yellow: as the meaning of the yellow light in sign E-5
signals.e8: Special traffic light before a bend, a general hazard, or a pedestrian crossing - flashing yellow, with or without a pedestrian figure: (a) proceed with caution, observing the signs; (b) if a pedestrian light is installed beside the crossing, the driver must allow pedestrians to cross the road safely
signals.e9: Pedestrian light beside a pedestrian crossing - red standing figure: crossing the road is prohibited, and anyone on the road must clear it immediately
signals.e10: Pedestrian light beside a pedestrian crossing - green walking figure: you may cross the road carefully at the pedestrian crossing
signals.e11: Double flashing red light: stop before the stop line; if there is none, before the nearest barrier; if there is no barrier, at a distance of at least 4 m from the nearest rail - until the flashing red light stops
signals.f1: Barrier before a railway crossing: if the barrier is descending or in a horizontal position, road users must stop before it
signals.f2: Barrier before a work site or a road closed by an authorized authority: the road is blocked. If the road is not blocked across its full width, road users may pass the barrier and continue carefully along the work area; if the whole road is blocked, road users may proceed only in the direction indicated by the appropriate sign
signals.f3: Curb stones painted black and white: to highlight edges, dividers, or built islands
signals.f7: Studs, with or without light reflectors, to highlight traffic arrangements or a road marking, according to the color of the marking
signals.f8: Pedestrian guard barrier: closes the road area to pedestrians; a pedestrian may not enter the road area blocked by the barrier
signals.f9: Warning and guidance sign at a branching junction: the driver must slow down and continue in the directions marked by the arrows on the sign. At roadwork sites the colors are orange and white
signals.f10: Warning and guidance sign at a sharp bend: the driver must slow down and continue in the direction marked by the arrows on the sign. At roadwork sites the colors are orange and white
